|  | [SwitchVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/SwitchVar.html) | `$SW{arg,pattern1:then1[,pattern2:then2...]}` |
|  | [CoalesceVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/CoalesceVar.html) | `$CO{arg1[,arg2...]}` |
|  | [PatternMatchVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/PatternMatchVar.html) | `$PM{arg,pattern}` |
|  | [PatternReplaceVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/PatternReplaceVar.html) | `$PR{arg,pattern,replace}` |
|  | [PatternExtractVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/PatternExtractVar.html) | `$PE{arg,pattern,groupdIndex}` |
|  | [NotEmptyVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/NotEmptyVar.html) | `$NE{arg}` |
|  | [UpperCaseVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/UpperCaseVar.html) | `$UC{arg}` |
|  | [LowerCaseVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/LowerCaseVar.html) | `$LC{arg}` |
|  | [LenVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/LenVar.html) | `$LN{arg[,delimiter]}` |
|  | [SubstringVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/SubstringVar.html) | `$ST{arg,start[,end]}` |
|  | [HtmlWidgetVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlWidgetVar.html) | `$W{name}` |
| **juneau-config** | [ConfigVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/config/vars/ConfigVar.html) | `$C{key[,default]}` |
| **juneau-rest-server** | [FileVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/FileVar.html) | `$F{path[,default]}` |
|  | [ServletInitParamVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/ServletInitParamVar.html) | `$I{name[,default]}` |
|  | [LocalizationVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/LocalizationVar.html) | `$L{key[,args...]}` |
|  | [RequestAttributeVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/RequestAttributeVar.html) | `$RA{key1[,key2...]}` |
|  | [RequestFormDataVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/RequestFormDataVar.html) | `$RF{key1[,key2...]}` |
|  | [RequestHeaderVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/RequestHeaderVar.html) | `$RH{key1[,key2...]}` |
|  | [RequestPathVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/RequestPathVar.html) | `$RP{key1[,key2...]}` |
|  | [RequestQueryVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/RequestQueryVar.html) | `$RQ{key1[,key2...]}` |
|  | [RequestSwaggerVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/RequestSwaggerVar.html) | `$RS{key}` |
|  | [RequestVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/RequestVar.html) | `$R{key1[,key2...]}` |
|  | [SerializedRequestAttrVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/SerializedRequestAttrVar.html) | `$SA{contentType,key[,default]}` |
|  | [SwaggerVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/SwaggerVar.html) | `$SS{key1[,key2...]}` |
|  | [UrlVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/UrlVar.html) | `$U{uri}` |
|  | [UrlEncodeVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/vars/UrlEncodeVar.html) | `$UE{uriPart}` |

---

<a id="topics-varresolvers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/VarResolvers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 51 -->

# VarResolvers and VarResolverSessions

The main class for performing variable resolution is [VarResolver](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.html).

Two methods are provided for resolving variables:

[VarResolver](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.html)
[resolve(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.html#resolve(java.lang.String))[resolveTo(String,Writer)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.html#resolveTo(java.lang.String,java.io.Writer))

Var resolvers can rely on the existence of other objects.
For example, [ConfigVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/config/vars/ConfigVar.html) relies on the existence of a [Config](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/config/Config.html).
This is accomplished through the following method:

[VarResolver.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.Builder.html)
[bean(Class<T>, T)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.Builder.html#bean(java.lang.Class,java.lang.Object)) - Specify a bean for all sessions.[VarResolverSession](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolverSession.html)
[bean(Class<T>, T)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolverSession.html#bean(java.lang.Class,T)) - Specify a bean for this session.

Beans are accessible through the following method:

[VarResolverSession.getBean(Class)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolverSession.html#getBean(java.lang.Class))

Var resolvers can be cloned and extended by using the [VarResolver.copy()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.html#copy()) method.
Cloning a resolver will copy it's [Var](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/Var.html) class names and context objects.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Create a resolver that copies the default resolver and adds $C and $A vars.
> VarResolver myVarResolver = VarResolver
>     .DEFAULT
>     .copy()
>     .vars(ConfigVar.class, ArgsVar.class)
>     .build();
> ```

---

<a id="topics-defaultvarresolver"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/DefaultVarResolver/ -->

<!-- page_index: 52 -->

# VarResolver.DEFAULT

[VarResolver.DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.html#DEFAULT) is a reusable variable resolver with
default support for the following variables:

- `$S{key[,default]}` - [SystemPropertiesVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/SystemPropertiesVar.html)
- `$E{key[,default]}` - [EnvVariablesVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/EnvVariablesVar.html)
- `$A{key[,default]}` - [ArgsVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/ArgsVar.html)
- `$MF{key[,default]}` - [ManifestFileVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/ManifestFileVar.html)
- `$SW{stringArg,pattern:thenValue[,pattern:thenValue...]}` - [SwitchVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/SwitchVar.html)
- `$IF{arg,then[,else]}` - [IfVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/IfVar.html)
- `$CO{arg[,arg2...]}` - [CoalesceVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/CoalesceVar.html)
- `$PM{arg,pattern}` - [PatternMatchVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/PatternMatchVar.html)
- `$PR{stringArg,pattern,replace}`- [PatternReplaceVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/PatternReplaceVar.html)
- `$PE{arg,pattern,groupIndex}` - [PatternExtractVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/PatternExtractVar.html)
- `$UC{arg}` - [UpperCaseVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/UpperCaseVar.html)
- `$LC{arg}` - [LowerCaseVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/LowerCaseVar.html)
- `$NE{arg}` - [NotEmptyVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/NotEmptyVar.html)
- `$LN{arg[,delimiter]}` - [LenVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/LenVar.html)
- `$ST{arg,start[,end]}` - [SubstringVar](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/vars/SubstringVar.html)

---

<a id="topics-svlothernotes"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SvlOtherNotes/ -->

<!-- page_index: 53 -->

# Other Notes

> [!WARNING]
> It is possible to cause [StackOverflowErrors](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/StackOverflowError.html) if your nested variables
> result in a recursive loop (e.g. the environment variable `'MYPROPERTY'` has the value `'$E{MYPROPERTY}'`).
> So don't do that!

---

<a id="topics-marshallencoders"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MarshallEncoders/ -->

<!-- page_index: 54 -->

# Encoders

The [org.apache.juneau.encoders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/package-summary.html) package defines an API for handling encoding-based matching
of `Accept-Encoding`/`Content-Encoding` HTTP headers.

It consists of the following classes:

[EncoderSet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/EncoderSet.html)
[Encoder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/Encoder.html)
[IdentityEncoder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/IdentityEncoder.html)
[GzipEncoder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/GzipEncoder.html)

The [EncoderSet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/EncoderSet.html) class represents the set of [org.apache.juneau.encoders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/Encoder.html) keyed by codings.
It maintains a set of encoders and the codings that they can handle.

The [getEncoderMatch(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/EncoderSet.html#getEncoderMatch(java.lang.String)) and [EncoderSet.getEncoder(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/EncoderSet.html#getEncoder(java.lang.String)) methods are then used to find appropriate encoders for specific `Accept-Encoding` and `Content-Encoding` header values.

Encoders are tried in the order they appear in the set.
The [EncoderSet.Builder.add(Class...)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/EncoderSet.Builder.html#add(java.lang.Class...)) / [EncoderSet.Builder.add(Encoder...)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/EncoderSet.Builder.html#add(org.apache.juneau.encoders.Encoder...)) methods prepend the values to the list to allow them the opportunity to override encoders already in the list.

For example, calling `builder.add(E1.class,E2.class).add(E3.class, E4.class)` will result in the order `E3, E4, E1, E2`.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Create an encoder group with support for gzip compression.
> EncoderSet encoders = EncoderSet
>     .create()
>     .add(GzipEncoder.class)
>     .build();
>
> // Should return "gzip"
> String matchedCoding = encoders.findMatch("compress;q=1.0, gzip;q=0.8, identity;q=0.5, *;q=0");
>
> // Get the encoder
> Encoder encoder = encoders.getEncoder(matchedCoding);
> ```

The [Encoder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/Encoder.html) interface is used for enabling decompression on
requests and compression on responses, such as support for GZIP compression.
It is used to wrap input and output streams within compression/decompression streams.

[Encoder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/Encoder.html)
[getCodings()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/Encoder.html#getCodings())[getInputStream(InputStream)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/Encoder.html#getInputStream(java.io.InputStream))[getOutputStream(OutputStream)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/encoders/Encoder.html#getOutputStream(java.io.OutputStream))

> [!NOTE]
> Encoders are registered with [RestServlets](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html) through the [@Rest(encoders)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#encoders()) annotation.

---

<a id="topics-objecttools"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ObjectTools/ -->

<!-- page_index: 55 -->

# Object Tools

The [org.apache.juneau.objecttools](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/package-summary.html) package defines convenience utility
classes for accessing and manipulating POJOs.

It consists of the following classes:

[ObjectRest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectRest.html)
[ObjectSearcher](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectSearcher.html)
[ObjectSorter](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectSorter.html)
[ObjectViewer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectViewer.html)
[ObjectPaginator](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectPaginator.html)
[ObjectIntrospector](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectIntrospector.html)
[ObjectMerger](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectMerger.html)

The [ObjectRest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectRest.html) class provides the ability to perform
standard REST operations (GET, PUT, POST, DELETE) against nodes in a POJO model.
Nodes in the POJO model are addressed using URLs.

A POJO model is defined as a tree model where nodes consist of consisting of the following:

[Maps](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/Map.html) and Java beans representing JSON objects.[Collections](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/Collection.html) and arrays representing JSON arrays.Java beans.

Leaves of the tree can be any type of object.

- Use [get()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectRest.html#get(java.lang.String)) to retrieve an element from a JSON tree.
- Use [put()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectRest.html#put(java.lang.String,java.lang.Object)) to create (or overwrite) an element in a JSON tree.
- Use [post()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectRest.html#post(java.lang.String,java.lang.Object)) to add an element to a list in a JSON tree.
- Use [delete()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectRest.html#delete(java.lang.String)) to remove an element from a JSON tree.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Construct an unstructured POJO model
> JsonMap map = JsonMap.ofJson(""
>     + "{"
>     + "    name:'John Smith', "
>     + "    address:{ "
>     + "        streetAddress:'21 2nd Street', "
>     + "        city:'New York', "
>     + "        state:'NY', "
>     + "        postalCode:10021 "
>     + "    }, "
>     + "    phoneNumbers:[ "
>     + "        '212 555-1111', "
>     + "        '212 555-2222' "
>     + "    ], "
>     + "    additionalInfo:null, "
>     + "    remote:false, "
>     + "    height:62.4, "
>     + "    'fico score':' > 640' "
>     + "} "
>
> );
>
> // Wrap Map inside an ObjectRest object
> ObjectRest johnSmith = ObjectRest.create(map);
>
> // Get a simple value at the top level
> // "John Smith"
> String name = johnSmith.getString("name");
>
> // Change a simple value at the top level
> johnSmith.put("name", "The late John Smith");
>
> // Get a simple value at a deep level
> // "21 2nd Street"
> String streetAddress = johnSmith.getString("address/streetAddress");
>
> // Set a simple value at a deep level
> johnSmith.put("address/streetAddress", "101 Cemetery Way");
>
> // Get entries in a list
> // "212 555-1111"
> String firstPhoneNumber = johnSmith.getString("phoneNumbers/0");
>
> // Add entries to a list
> johnSmith.post("phoneNumbers", "212 555-3333");
>
> // Delete entries from a model
> johnSmith.delete("fico score");
>
> // Add entirely new structures to the tree
> JsonMap medicalInfo = JsonMap.ofJson(""
>     + "{"
>     + "	currentStatus: 'deceased',"
>     + "	health: 'non-existent',"
>     + "	creditWorthiness: 'not good'"
>     + "}"
>
> );
> johnSmith.put("additionalInfo/medicalInfo", medicalInfo);
> ```

In the special case of `Collections`/arrays of `Maps`/beans, a special XPath-like selector notation can be used in lieu of
index numbers on GET requests to return a map/bean with a specified attribute value.
The syntax is `@attr=val`, where attr is the attribute name on the child map, and val is the matching value.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Get map/bean with name attribute value of 'foo' from a list of items
> Map map = objectRest.getMap("/items/@name=foo");
> ```

> [!NOTE]
> This class is used in the [Traversable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/converter/Traversable.html) REST response
> converter.

The [ObjectSearcher](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectSearcher.html) class is designed to provide
searches across arrays and `Collections` of `Maps` or beans.
It allows you to quickly filter beans and `Maps` using simple yet sophisticated search arguments.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> MyBean[] arrayOfBeans = ...;
> ObjectSearcher searcher = ObjectSearcher.create();
>
> // Returns a list of beans whose 'foo' property is 'X' and 'bar' property is 'Y'.
> List result = searcher.run(arrayOfBeans, "foo=X,bar=Y");
> ```

The tool can be used against the following data types:

- Arrays/`Collections` of `Maps` or beans.

The default searcher is configured with the following matcher factories that provides the capabilities of matching
against various data types.
This list is extensible:

[StringMatcherFactory](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/StringMatcherFactory.html)
[NumberMatcherFactory](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/NumberMatcherFactory.html)
[TimeMatcherFactory](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/TimeMatcherFactory.html)

The [StringMatcherFactory](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/StringMatcherFactory.html) class provides
searching based on the following patterns:

- `"property=foo"` - Simple full word match
- `"property=fo*"`, `"property=?ar"` - Meta-character matching
- `"property=foo bar"`(implicit), `"property=^foo ^bar"`(explicit) - Multiple OR'ed patterns
- `"property=+fo* +*ar"` - Multiple AND'ed patterns
- `"property=fo* -bar"` - Negative patterns
- `"property='foo bar'"` - Patterns with whitespace
- `"property=foo\\'bar"` - Patterns with single-quotes
- `"property=/foo\\s+bar"` - Regular expression match

The [NumberMatcherFactory](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/NumberMatcherFactory.html) class provides
searching based on the following patterns:

- `"property=1"` - A single number
- `"property=1 2"` - Multiple OR'ed numbers
- `"property=-1 -2"` - Multiple OR'ed negative numbers
- `"property=1-2"`, `"property=-2--1"` - A range of numbers (whitespace ignored)
- `"property=1-2 4-5"` - Multiple OR'ed ranges
- `"property=<1"`, `"property=<=1"`, `"property=>1"`, `"property=>=1"` - Open-ended ranges
- `"property=!1"`, `"property=!1-2"` - Negation

The [TimeMatcherFactory](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/TimeMatcherFactory.html) class provides searching
based on the following patterns:

- `"property=2011"` - A single year
- `"property=2011 2013 2015"` - Multiple years
- `"property=2011-01"` - A single month
- `"property=2011-01-01"` - A single day
- `"property=2011-01-01T12"` - A single hour
- `"property=2011-01-01T12:30"` - A single minute
- `"property=2011-01-01T12:30:45"` - A single second
- `"property=>2011"`, `"property=>=2011"`, `"property=<2011"`, `"property=<=2011"` - Open-ended ranges
- `"property=2011 - 2013-06-30"` - Closed ranges

> [!NOTE]
> This class is used in the [Queryable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/converter/Queryable.html) REST response
> converter.

The [ObjectSorter](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectSorter.html) class is designed to sort arrays and
collections of maps or beans.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> MyBean[] arrayOfBeans = ...;
> ObjectSorter sorter = ObjectSorter.create();
>
> // Returns a list of beans sorted accordingly.
> List result = sorter.run(arrayOfBeans, "foo,bar-");
> ```

The tool can be used against the following data types:

- Arrays/`Collections` of `Maps` or beans.

The arguments are a simple comma-delimited list of property names optionally suffixed with '+' and '-' to
denote ascending/descending order.

> [!NOTE]
> This class is used in the [Queryable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/converter/Queryable.html) REST response
> converter.

The [ObjectViewer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectViewer.html) class is designed to extract properties
from `Collections` of `Maps` or beans.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> MyBean[] arrayOfBeans = ...;
> ObjectViewer viewer = ObjectViewer.create();
> // Returns the 'foo' and 'bar' properties extracted into a list of maps.
> List result = viewer.run(arrayOfBeans, "foo,bar");
> ```

The tool can be used against the following data types:

- Arrays/`Collections` of `Maps` or beans.
- Singular maps or beans.

> [!NOTE]
> This class is used in the [Queryable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/converter/Queryable.html) REST response
> converter.

The [ObjectPaginator](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectPaginator.html) class is designed to extract
sublists from arrays/collections of maps or beans.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> MyBean[] arrayOfBeans = ...;
> ObjectPaginator paginator = ObjectPaginator.create();
>
> // Returns all rows from 100 to 110.
> List result = paginator.run(arrayOfBeans, 100, 10);
> ```

The tool can be used against the following data types:

- Arrays/`Collections` of `Maps` or beans.

> [!NOTE]
> This class is used in the [Queryable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/converter/Queryable.html) REST response
> converter.

The [ObjectIntrospector](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectIntrospector.html) class is used to invoke
methods on `Objects` using arguments in serialized form.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> String string1 = "foobar";
> String string2 = ObjectIntrospector
>     .create(string)
>     .invoke(String.class, "substring(int,int)", "[3,6]");  // "bar"
> ```

The arguments passed to the identified method are POJOs serialized in JSON format.
Arbitrarily complex arguments can be passed
in as arguments.

> [!NOTE]
> This class is used in the [Introspectable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/converter/Introspectable.html) REST
> response converter.

> [!WARNING]
> This is an extremely powerful but potentially dangerous tool.
> Use wisely.

The [ObjectMerger](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/objecttools/ObjectMerger.html) class is used for merging POJOs behind
a single interface.
This is particularly useful in cases where you want to define beans with 'default' values.
For example, given the following bean classes:

```java
public interface IA { 
    String getX(); 
    void setX(String x); 
} 
 
public class A implements IA { 
    private String x; 
 
    public A(String x) { 
        this.x = x; 
    } 
 
    public String getX() { 
        return x; 
    } 
 
    public void setX(String x) { 
        this.x = x; 
    } 
} 
```

The getters will be called in order until the first non-null value is returned:

```java
merge = ObjectMerger.merger(IA.class, new A("1"), new A("2")); 
assertEquals("1", merge.getX()); 
 
merge = ObjectMerger.merger(IA.class, new A(null), new A("2")); 
assertEquals("2", merge.getX()); 
 
merge = ObjectMerger.merger(IA.class, new A(null), new A(null)); 
assertEquals(null, merge.getX()); 
```

---

<a id="topics-jsonbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 56 -->

# JSON Basics

Juneau supports converting arbitrary POJOs to and from JSON using ultra-efficient serializers and parsers.
The JSON serializer converts POJOs directly to JSON without the need for intermediate DOM objects using a
highly-efficient state machine.
Likewise, the [JSON parser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonParser.html) creates POJOs directly from JSON without the need for intermediate DOM objects.

The following example shows JSON for a typical bean:

```java
public class Person { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String name; 
    @Swap(TemporalCalendarSwap.IsoInstant.class) public Calendar birthDate; 
    public List addresses; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
 
public class Address { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String street, city; 
    public StateEnum state; 
    public int zip; 
    public boolean isCurrent; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
```

```java
Person person = new Person() 
    .name("John Smith") 
    .birthDate("1946-08-12T00:00:00Z") 
    .addresses( 
        new Address() 
            .street("100 Main Street") 
            .city("Anywhereville") 
            .state(NY) 
            .zip(12345) 
            .isCurrent(true); 
    ); 
```

```json
{ 
    "name": "John Smith", 
    "birthDate": "1946-08-12T00:00:00Z", 
    "addresses": [ 
        { 
            "street": "100 Main Street", 
            "city": "Anywhereville", 
            "state": "NY", 
            "zip": 12345, 
            "isCurrent": true 
        } 
    ] 
} 
```

```js
{ 
    name: 'John Smith', 
    birthDate: '1946-08-12T00:00:00Z', 
    addresses: [ 
        { 
            street: '100 Main Street', 
            city: 'Anywhereville', 
            state: 'NY', 
            zip: 12345, 
            isCurrent: true 
        } 
    ] 
} 
```

---

<a id="topics-jsonmethodology"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonMethodology/ -->

<!-- page_index: 57 -->

# JSON Methodology

The JSON data type produced depends on the Java object type being serialized.

- Primitives and primitive objects are converted to JSON primitives.
- Beans and `Maps` are converted to JSON objects.
- `Collections` and arrays are converted to JSON arrays.
- Anything else is converted to JSON strings.

| POJO type | JSON type | Example | Serialized form |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| String | String | `serialize("foobar");` | `'foobar'` |
| Number | Number | `serialize(123);` | `123` |
| Boolean | Boolean | `serialize(true);` | `true` |
| Null | Null | `serialize(null);` | `null` |
| Beans with properties of any type on this list | Object | `serialize(new MyBean());` | `{p1:'val1',p2:true}` |
| `Maps` with values of any type on this list | Object | `serialize(new TreeMap());` | `{key1:'val1',key2:true}` |
| `Collections` and arrays of any type on this list | Array | `serialize(new Object[]{1,"foo",true});` | `[1,'foo',true]` |

In addition, swaps can be used to convert non-serializable POJOs into serializable forms, such as converting `Calendar`
object to ISO8601 strings, or `byte[]` arrays to Base-64 encoded strings.

---

<a id="topics-jsonserializers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonSerializers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 58 -->

# JSON Serializers

The [JsonSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.html) class is used to serialize POJOs into
JSON.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this serializer is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[BeanTraverseContext.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanTraverseContext.Builder.html)
[Serializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/Serializer.Builder.html)
[WriterSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/WriterSerializer.Builder.html)
[JsonSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured serializers are provided for convenience:

[JsonSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.html#DEFAULT)
[DEFAULT\_READABLE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.html#DEFAULT_READABLE)
[Json5Serializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/Json5Serializer.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/Json5Serializer.html#DEFAULT)
[DEFAULT\_READABLE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/Json5Serializer.html#DEFAULT_READABLE)

---

<a id="topics-json5"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Json5/ -->

<!-- page_index: 59 -->

# JSON 5

The [Json5Serializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/Json5Serializer.html) class can be used to serialized POJOs
into JSON 5 notation.

JSON 5 is similar to JSON except for the following:

- JSON attributes are only quoted when necessary.
- Uses single-quotes for quoting.

> [!TIP]
> **Examples**
> ```java
> // Some free-form JSON.
> JsonMap map = JsonMap.of(
>     "foo", "x1",
>     "_bar", "x2",
>     " baz ", "x3",
>     "123", "x4",
>     "return", "x5",
>     "", "x6"
> );
> ```
>
> ```js
> // Serialized to standard JSON
> {
>     "foo": "x1",
>     "_bar": "x2",
>     " baz ": "x3",
>     "123": "x4",
>     "return": "x5",
>     "": "x6"
> }
> ```
>
> ```js
> // Serialized to JSON5
> {
>     foo: 'x1',
>     _bar: 'x2',
>     ' baz ': 'x3',  // Quoted due to embedded spaces.
>     '123': 'x4',    // Quoted to prevent confusion with number.
>     'return': 'x5', // Quoted because it's a keyword.
>     '': 'x6'        // Quoted because it's an empty string.
> }
> ```

The advantage to JSON 5 is you can represent it in a Java String in minimal form with minimal escaping.
This is particularly useful in cases such as unit testing where you can easily validate POJOs by simplifying them to
JSON 5 and do a simple string comparison.

```java
WriterSerializer serializer = Json5Serializer.DEFAULT; 
assertEquals("{foo:'bar',baz:123}", serializer.toString(myPojo)); 
```

> [!NOTE]
> **See Also**
> [JsonSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.Builder.html)
> [json5()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.Builder.html#json5())

---

<a id="topics-jsonparsers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonParsers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 60 -->

# JSON Parsers

The [JsonParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonParser.html) class is used to parse JSON into POJOs.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this parser is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[Parser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/Parser.Builder.html)
[ReaderParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/ReaderParser.Builder.html)
[JsonParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonParser.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured parsers are provided for convenience:

[JsonParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonParser.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonParser.html#DEFAULT)
[DEFAULT\_STRICT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonParser.html#DEFAULT_STRICT)

The [JSON parser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonParser.html) supports ALL valid JSON, including:

- Javascript comments.
- Single or double quoted values.
- Quoted (strict) or unquoted (non-strict) attributes.
- JSON fragments (such as string, numeric, or boolean primitive values).
- Concatenated strings.

---

<a id="topics-jsonannotation"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonAnnotation/ -->

<!-- page_index: 61 -->

# @Json Annotation

The [@Json](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/annotation/Json.html) annotation is used to override the behavior of [JsonSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.html) on individual bean classes or properties.

The annotation can be applied to beans as well as other objects serialized to other types (e.g. strings).

[Json](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/annotation/Json.html)
[wrapperAttr](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/annotation/Json.html#wrapperAttr())

The [@Json(wrapperAttr)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/annotation/Json.html#wrapperAttr()) annotation can be used
to wrap beans inside a JSON object with a specified attribute name.

> [!TIP]
> **Example:**
> ```java
> @Json(wrapperAttr="personBean")
>
> public class Person {
>     public String name = "John Smith";
> }
> ```

The following shows the JSON representation with and without the annotation present:

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Without annotation</th><th>With annotation</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>"name"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"John Smith"</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>"personBean"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>"name"</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>"John Smith"</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>}</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

---

<a id="topics-jsonschemadetails"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JsonSchemaDetails/ -->

<!-- page_index: 62 -->

# JSON-Schema Support

Juneau provides the [JsonSchemaSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSchemaSerializer.html) class for
generating JSON-Schema documents that describe the output generated by the [JsonSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.html) class.
This class shares the same properties as `JsonSerializer`.

For convenience the [JsonSerializer.getSchemaSerializer()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.html#getSchemaSerializer()) method has been added for creating instances of schema serializers from the regular serializer instance.

```java
public class Person { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String name; 
    public Calendar birthDate; 
    public List addresses; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
 
public class Address { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String street, city; 
    public StateEnum state; 
    public int zip; 
    public boolean isCurrent; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
```

The code for creating our POJO model and generating JSON-Schema is shown below:

```java
// Get the one of the default schema serializers. 
JsonSchemaSerializer serializer = JsonSchemaSerializer.DEFAULT_SIMPLE_READABLE; 
 
// Get the JSON Schema for the POJO. 
String jsonSchema = serializer.serialize(new Person()); 
 
// This also works. 
jsonSchema = serializer.serialize(Person.class); 
```

```js
{ 
    type: 'object', 
    description: 'org.apache.juneau.sample.Person', 
    properties: { 
        name: { 
            type: 'string', 
            description: 'java.lang.String' 
        }, 
        birthDate: { 
            type: 'string', 
            description: 'java.util.Calendar' 
        }, 
        addresses: { 
            type: 'array', 
            description: 'java.util.LinkedList', 
            items: { 
                type: 'object', 
                description: 'org.apache.juneau.sample.Address', 
                properties: { 
                    street: { 
                        type: 'string', 
                        description: 'java.lang.String' 
                    }, 
                    city: { 
                        type: 'string', 
                        description: 'java.lang.String' 
                    }, 
                    state: { 
                        type: 'string', 
                        description: 'java.lang.String' 
                    }, 
                    zip: { 
                        type: 'number', 
                        description: 'int' 
                    }, 
                    isCurrent: { 
                        type: 'boolean', 
                        description: 'boolean' 
                    } 
                } 
            } 
        } 
    } 
} 
```

---

<a id="topics-xmlbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/XmlBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 63 -->

# XML Basics

Juneau supports converting arbitrary POJOs to and from XML using ultra-efficient serializers and parsers.

The XML serializer converts POJOs directly to XML without the need for intermediate DOM objects.
Likewise, the XML parser uses a STaX parser and creates POJOs directly without intermediate DOM objects.

Unlike frameworks such as JAXB, Juneau does not require POJO classes to be annotated to produce and consume XML.
However, several XML annotations are provided for handling namespaces and fine-tuning the format of the XML produced.

The following example shows XML for a typical bean:

```java
@Bean(typeName="person") 
public class Person { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String name; 
    @Swap(TemporalCalendarSwap.IsoInstant.class) public Calendar birthDate; 
    public List addresses; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
 
@Bean(typeName="address") 
public class Address { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String street, city; 
    public StateEnum state; 
    public int zip; 
    public boolean isCurrent; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
```

```java
Person person = new Person() 
    .name("John Smith") 
    .birthDate("1946-08-12T00:00:00Z") 
    .addresses( 
        new Address() 
            .street("100 Main Street") 
            .city("Anywhereville") 
            .state(NY) 
            .zip(12345) 
            .isCurrent(true); 
    ); 
```

```xml
<person> 
    <name>John Smith</name> 
    <birthDate>1946-08-12T04:00:00Z</birthDate> 
    <addresses> 
        <address> 
            <street>100 Main Street</street> 
            <city>Anywhereville</city> 
            <state>NY</state> 
            <zip>12345</zip> 
            <isCurrent>true</isCurrent> 
        </address> 
    </addresses> 
</person> 
```

Juneau produces JSON-equivalent XML, meaning any valid JSON document can be losslessly converted into an XML equivalent.
In fact, all of the Juneau serializers and parsers are built upon this JSON-equivalence.

---

<a id="topics-xmlmethodology"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/XmlMethodology/ -->

<!-- page_index: 64 -->

# XML Methodology

The following examples show how different data types are represented in XML.
They mirror how the data structures are represented in JSON.

The representation of loose (not a direct bean property value) simple types are shown below:

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>XML</th></tr><tr><td>string</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>'foo'</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>boolean</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>true</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span><span>true</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>integer</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>123</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>float</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>1.23</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>1.23</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>null</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>null</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>/&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

Loose `maps` and beans use the element `<object>` for encapsulation.
`_type` attributes are added to bean properties or map entries if the type cannot be inferred through reflection (e.g.
an `Object` or superclass/interface value type).

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>XML</th></tr><tr><td>Map</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k1</span><span>:</span><span> 'v1'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k2</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k1</span><span>&gt;</span><span>v1</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k1</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k2</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>null</span><span>'</span><span>/&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Map</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k1</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k2</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k3</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k1</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k1</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k2</span><span>&gt;</span><span>1.23</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k2</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k3</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>null</span><span>'</span><span>/&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Map</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k1</span><span>:</span><span> 'v1'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k2</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k3</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k4</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>true</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k5</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k1</span><span>&gt;</span><span>v1</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k1</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k2</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>number</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k2</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k3</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>number</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span>1.23</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k3</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k4</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>boolean</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span>true</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k4</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k5</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>null</span><span>'</span><span>/&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

Loose `collections` and arrays use the element `<array>` for encapsulation.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>XML</th></tr><tr><td>String[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>'foo'</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>/&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Number[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>1.23</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>/&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Object[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>'foo'</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>true</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>1.23</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span><span>true</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>/&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>String[][]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>[</span><span>'foo'</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>/&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>/&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>int[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>123</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>boolean[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>true</span><span>]</span> </span><span>&lt;</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span><span>true</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>List&lt;String&gt;</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>'foo'</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>List&lt;Number&gt;</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>true</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>array</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> <table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>XML</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>int</span><span> b</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>Object</span><span> c</span><span>;</span><span>  </span><span>// String value</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>Object</span><span> d</span><span>;</span><span> h</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> 'foo'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  b</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  c</span><span>:</span><span> 'bar'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  d</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>456</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  e</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    h</span><span>:</span><span> 'baz'</span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>}</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  f</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>[</span><span>'qux'</span><span>]</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  g</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>[</span><span>789</span><span>]</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>c</span><span>&gt;</span><span>bar</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>c</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>d</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>number</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span>456</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>d</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>e</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>
