</span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>boolean</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span>true</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k5</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k6</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>null</span><span>'</span><span>/&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>c</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

---

<a id="topics-xmlserializers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/XmlSerializers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 65 -->

# XML Serializers

The [XmlSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html) class is used to serialize POJOs into XML.

The [XmlDocSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlDocSerializer.html) class is the same but serializes a
`<?xml?>` header at the top of the file.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this serializer is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[BeanTraverseContext.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanTraverseContext.Builder.html)
[Serializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/Serializer.Builder.html)
[WriterSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/WriterSerializer.Builder.html)
[XmlSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured serializers are provided for convenience:

[XmlSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT) [DEFAULT\_JUNEAU\_NAMESPACE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT_JUNEAU_NAMESPACE) [DEFAULT\_NS](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT_NS) [DEFAULT\_NS\_SQ](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT_NS_SQ) [DEFAULT\_NS\_SQ\_READABLE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT_NS_SQ_READABLE) [DEFAULT\_SQ](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT_SQ) [DEFAULT\_SQ\_READABLE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT_SQ_READABLE) [DEFAULT\_XS\_NAMESPACE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT_XS_NAMESPACE)

---

<a id="topics-xmlparsers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/XmlParsers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 66 -->

# XML Parsers

The [XmlParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlParser.html) class is used to parse XML into POJOs.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this parser is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[Parser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/Parser.Builder.html)
[ReaderParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/ReaderParser.Builder.html)
[XmlParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlParser.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured parsers are provided for convenience:

[XmlParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlParser.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlParser.html#DEFAULT)

---

<a id="topics-xmlbeantypenameannotation"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/XmlBeanTypeNameAnnotation/ -->

<!-- page_index: 67 -->

# @Bean(typeName) Annotation

The [@Bean(typeName)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/annotation/Bean.html#typeName()) annotation can be used to override the Juneau default name on bean elements.

Types names serve two distinct purposes:

1. To override the element name.
2. To serve as a class identifier so that the bean class can be inferred during parsing if it cannot automatically be inferred through reflection.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> | Data type | JSON example | Without annotation | With annotation |
> | --- | --- | --- | --- |
> |
> ```java
> @Bean(typeName="X")
> class MyBean {
>   public String a;
>   public int b;
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```json
> {
>   a: 'foo',
>   b: 123
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>foo</a>
>   <b>123</b>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <X>
>   <a>foo</a>
>   <b>123</b>
> </X>
> ```
>  |

On bean properties, a `_type` attribute will be added if a type name is present and the bean class cannot be inferred through reflection.

In the following example, a type attribute is used on property 'b' but not property 'a' since 'b' is of type `Object` and therefore the bean class cannot be inferred.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> | Java | Without annotation | With annotation |
> | --- | --- | --- |
> |
> ```java
> class MyBean {
>   public BeanX a = new BeanX();
>   public Object b = new BeanX();
> }
>
> @Bean(typeName="X")
> class BeanX {
>   public String fx = "foo";
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <fx>foo</fx>
>   </a>
>   <b>
>     <fx>foo</fx>
>   </b>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <fx>foo</fx>
>   </a>
>   <b _type='X'>
>     <fx>foo</fx>
>   </b>
> </object>
> ```
>  |

> [!NOTE]
> **info**
> `string`, `number`, `boolean`, `object`, `array`, and `null` are reserved keywords that cannot be used as type names.

Beans with type names are often used in conjunction with the [@Bean(dictionary)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/annotation/Bean.html#dictionary()) and [@Beanp(dictionary)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/annotation/Beanp.html#dictionary()) annotations so that the beans can be resolved at parse time.
These annotations are not necessary during serialization but are needed during parsing in order to resolve the bean types.

The following examples show how type names are used under various circumstances.
Pay special attention to when `_type` attributes are and are not used.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> | Java | XML |
> | --- | --- |
> |
> ```java
> @Bean(dictionary={BeanX.class})
> class BeanWithArrayPropertiesWithTypeNames {
>   public BeanX[] b1 = new BeanX[]{
>     new BeanX()
>   };
>   public Object[] b2 = new BeanX[]{
>     new BeanX()
>   };
>   public Object[] b3 = new Object[]{
>     new BeanX()
>   };
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <b1>
>     <X>
>       <fx>foo</fx>
>     </X>
>   </b1>
>   <b2>
>     <X>
>       <fx>foo</fx>
>     </X>
>   </b2>
>   <b3>
>     <X>
>       <fx>foo</fx>
>     </X>
>   </b3>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> |
> ```java
> @Bean(dictionary={BeanX.class})
> class BeanWith2dArrayPropertiesWithTypeNames {
>   public BeanX[][] b1 = new BeanX[][]{{
>     new BeanX()
>   }};
>   public Object[][] b2 = new BeanX[][]{{
>     new BeanX()
>   }};
>   public Object[][] b3 = new Object[][]{{
>     new BeanX()
>   }};
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <b1>
>     <array>
>       <X>
>         <fx>foo</fx>
>       </X>
>     </array>
>   </b1>
>   <b2>
>     <array>
>       <X>
>         <fx>foo</fx>
>       </X>
>     </array>
>   </b2>
>   <b3>
>     <array>
>       <X>
>         <fx>foo</fx>
>       </X>
>     </array>
>   </b3>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> |
> ```java
> @Bean(dictionary={BeanX.class})
> class BeanWithMapPropertiesWithTypeNames {
>   public Map<String,BeanX> b1 = new HashMap<>() {{
>     put("k1", new BeanX());
>   }};
>   public Map<String,Object> b2 = new HashMap<>() {{
>     put("k2", new BeanX());
>   }};
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <b1>
>     <k1>
>       <fx>foo</fx>
>     </k1>
>   </b1>
>   <b2>
>     <k2 _type='X'>
>       <fx>foo</fx>
>     </k2>
>   </b2>
> </object>
> ```
>  |

Bean type names are also used for resolution when abstract fields are used.
The following examples show how they are used in a variety of circumstances.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> | Java | XML |
> | --- | --- |
> |
> ```java
> @Bean(dictionary={A.class})
> class BeanWithAbstractFields {
>   public A a = new A();
>   public IA ia = new A();
>   public AA aa = new A();
>   public Object o = new A();
> }
>
> interface IA {}
>
> abstract class AA implements IA {}
>
> @Bean(typeName="A")
> class A extends AA {
>   public String fa = "foo";
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <fa>foo</fa>
>   </a>
>   <ia _type='A'>
>     <fa>foo</fa>
>   </ia>
>   <aa _type='A'>
>     <fa>foo</fa>
>   </aa>
>   <o _type='A'>
>     <fa>foo</fa>
>   </o>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> |
> ```java
> @Bean(dictionary={A.class})
> class BeanWithAbstractArrayFields {
>   public A[] a = new A[]{new A()};
>   public IA[] ia1 = new A[]{new A()};
>   public IA[] ia2 = new IA[]{new A()};
>   public AA[] aa1 = new A[]{new A()};
>   public AA[] aa2 = new AA[]{new A()};
>   public Object[] o1 = new A[]{new A()};
>   public Object[] o2 = new Object[]{new A()};
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <A>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </A>
>   </a>
>   <ia1>
>     <A>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </A>
>   </ia1>
>   <ia2>
>     <A>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </A>
>   </ia2>
>   <aa1>
>     <A>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </A>
>   </aa1>
>   <aa2>
>     <A>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </A>
>   </aa2>
>   <o1>
>     <A>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </A>
>   </o1>
>   <o2>
>     <A>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </A>
>   </o2>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> |
> ```java
> @Bean(dictionary={A.class})
> class BeanWithAbstractMapFields {
>   public Map<String,A> a = new HashMap<>() {{
>     put("k1", new A());
>   }};
>   public Map<String,AA> b = new HashMap<>() {{
>     put("k2", new A());
>   }};
>   public Map<String,Object> c = new HashMap<>() {{
>     put("k3", new A());
>   }};
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <k1>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </k1>
>   </a>
>   <b>
>     <k2 _type='A'>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </k2>
>   </b>
>   <c>
>     <k3 _type='A'>
>       <fa>foo</fa>
>     </k3>
>   </c>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> |
> ```java
> @Bean(dictionary={A.class})
> class BeanWithAbstractMapArrayFields {
>   public Map<String,A[]> a = new LinkedHashMap<>() {{
>     put("a1", new A[]{new A()});
>   }};
>   public Map<String,IA[]> ia = new LinkedHashMap<>() {{
>     put("ia1", new A[]{new A()});
>     put("ia2", new IA[]{new A()});
>   }};
>   public Map<String,AA[]> aa = new LinkedHashMap<>() {{
>     put("aa1", new A[]{new A()});
>     put("aa2", new AA[]{new A()});
>   }};
>   public Map<String,Object[]> o = new LinkedHashMap<>() {{
>     put("o1", new A[]{new A()});
>     put("o2", new AA[]{new A()});
>   }};
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <a1>
>       <A>
>         <fa>foo</fa>
>       </A>
>     </a1>
>   </a>
>   <ia>
>     <ia1>
>       <A>
>         <fa>foo</fa>
>       </A>
>     </ia1>
>     <ia2>
>       <A>
>         <fa>foo</fa>
>       </A>
>     </ia2>
>   </ia>
>   <aa>
>     <aa1>
>       <A>
>         <fa>foo</fa>
>       </A>
>     </aa1>
>     <aa2>
>       <A>
>         <fa>foo</fa>
>       </A>
>     </aa2>
>   </aa>
>   <o>
>     <o1>
>       <A>
>         <fa>foo</fa>
>       </A>
>     </o1>
>     <o2>
>       <A>
>         <fa>foo</fa>
>       </A>
>     </o2>
>   </o>
> </object>
> ```
>  |

On a side note, characters that cannot be represented in XML 1.0 are encoded using a simple encoding.
Note in the examples below, some characters such as `'\n'`, `'\t'`, and `'\r'` can be represented as XML entities when used in text but not in element names.
Other characters such as `'\b'` and `'\f'` cannot be encoded in XML 1.0 at all without inventing our own notation.
Whitespace characters in element names are encoded as well as whitespace end characters in text.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> | Java | XML |
> | --- | --- |
> |
> ```java
> class BeanWithSpecialCharacters {
>   public String a = "  \b\f\n\t\r  ";
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>_x0020_ _x0008__x000C_&#x000a;&#x0009;&#x000d; _x0020_</a>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> |
> ```java
> @Bean(typeName="  \b\f\n\t\r  ")
> class BeanWithNamesWithSpecialCharacters {
>   @Beanp(name="  \b\f\n\t\r  ")
>   public String a = "  \b\f\n\t\r  ";
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <_x0020__x0020__x0008__x000C__x000A__x0009__x000D__x0020__x0020_>
>   <_x0020__x0020__x0008__x000C__x000A__x0009__x000D__x0020__x0020_>
>     _x0020_ _x0008__x000C_&#x000a;&#x0009;&#x000d; _x0020_
>   </_x0020__x0020__x0008__x000C__x000A__x0009__x000D__x0020__x0020_>
> </_x0020__x0020__x0008__x000C__x000A__x0009__x000D__x0020__x0020_>
> ```
>  |

While it's true that these characters CAN be represented in XML 1.1, it's impossible to parse XML 1.1 text in Java without the XML containing an XML declaration.
Unfortunately, this, and the uselessness of the [XMLInputFactory.IS\_REPLACING\_ENTITY\_REFERENCES](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.xml/javax/xml/stream/XMLInputFactory.html#IS_REPLACING_ENTITY_REFERENCES) setting in Java forced us to make some hard design decisions that may not be the most elegant.

---

<a id="topics-xmlchildnameannotation"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/XmlChildNameAnnotation/ -->

<!-- page_index: 68 -->

# @Xml(childName) Annotation

The [@Xml(childName)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/Xml.html#childName()) annotation can be used to
specify the name of XML child elements for bean properties of type collection or array.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> | Data type | JSON example | Without annotation | With annotation |
> | --- | --- | --- | --- |
> |
> ```java
> class MyBean {
>   @Xml(childName="X")
>   public String[] a;
>   @Xml(childName="Y")
>   public int[] b;
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```json
> {
>   a: ['foo','bar'],
>   b: [123,456]
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <string>foo</string>
>     <string>bar</string>
>   </a>
>   <b>
>     <number>123</number>
>     <number>456</number>
>   </b>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <X>foo</X>
>     <X>bar</X>
>   </a>
>   <b>
>     <Y>123</Y>
>     <Y>456</Y>
>   </b>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> |
> ```java
> class MyBean {
>   @Xml(childName="child")
>   public int[] a;
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```json
> {
>   a: [123,456]
> }
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <number>123</number>
>     <number>456</number>
>   </a>
> </object>
> ```
>  |
> ```xml
> <object>
>   <a>
>     <child>123</child>
>     <child>456</child>
>   </a>
> </object>
> ```
>  |

---

<a id="topics-xmlformatannotation"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/XmlFormatAnnotation/ -->

<!-- page_index: 69 -->

# @Xml(format) Annotation

The [@Xml(format)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/Xml.html#format()) annotation can be used to tweak the
XML format of a POJO.
The value is set to an enum value of type [XmlFormat](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html).
This annotation can be applied to both classes and bean properties.
The [XmlFormat.ATTR](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#ATTR) format can be applied to bean
properties to serialize them as XML attributes instead of elements.
Note that this only supports properties of simple types (e.g.
strings, numbers, booleans).

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>Without annotation</th><th>With annotation</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ATTR</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> 'foo'</span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span> </span><span>a</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>foo</span><span>'</span><span>/&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

The [XmlFormat.ATTRS](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#ATTRS) format can be applied to bean
classes to force all bean properties to be serialized as XML attributes instead of child elements.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>Without annotation</th><th>With annotation</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ATTRS</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>int</span><span> b</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> 'foo'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  b</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span> </span><span>a</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>foo</span><span>'</span><span> </span><span>b</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>123</span><span>'</span><span>/&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

The [XmlFormat.ELEMENT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#ELEMENT) format can be applied to
bean properties to override the [XmlFormat.ATTRS](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#ATTRS)
format applied on the bean class.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>Without annotation</th><th>With annotation</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ATTRS</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ELEMENT</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>int</span><span> b</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> 'foo'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  b</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span> </span><span>a</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>foo</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

The [XmlFormat.ATTRS](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#ATTRS) format can be applied to a
single bean property of type `Map` to denote arbitrary XML attribute values on the element.
These can be mixed with other [XmlFormat.ATTR](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#ATTR)
annotated properties but there must not be an overlap in bean property names and map keys.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>Without annotation</th><th>With annotation</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ATTRS</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>Map</span><span>&lt;</span><span>String</span><span>,</span><span>Object</span><span>&gt;</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ATTR</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>int</span><span> b</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    k1</span><span>:</span><span> 'foo'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    k2</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>}</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  b</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>456</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k1</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k1</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>k2</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>number</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>k2</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span>456</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span> </span><span>k1</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>foo</span><span>'</span><span> </span><span>k2</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>123</span><span>'</span><span> </span><span>b</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>456</span><span>'</span><span>/&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

The [XmlFormat.COLLAPSED](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#COLLAPSED) format can be applied
to bean properties of type array/`Collection`.
This causes the child objects to be serialized directly inside the bean element.
This format must be used in conjunction with [@Xml(childName)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/Xml.html#childName()) to differentiate which collection the values came from if you plan on parsing the output back into beans.
Note that child names must not conflict with other property names.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>Without annotation</th><th>With annotation</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>childName</span><span>=</span><span>"A"</span><span>,</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>COLLAPSED</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span>[</span><span>]</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>childName</span><span>=</span><span>"B"</span><span>,</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>COLLAPSED</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>int</span><span>[</span><span>]</span><span> b</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>[</span><span>'foo'</span><span>,</span><span>'bar'</span><span>]</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  b</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>[</span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span>456</span><span>]</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span>bar</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>456</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>A</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>A</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>A</span><span>&gt;</span><span>bar</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>A</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>B</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>B</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>B</span><span>&gt;</span><span>456</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>B</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

The [XmlFormat.ELEMENTS](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#ELEMENTS) format can be applied to
a single bean property of either a simple type or array/`Collection`.
It allows free-form child elements to be formed.
All other properties on the bean MUST be serialized as attributes.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>With annotation</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ATTR</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ELEMENTS</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> b</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> 'foo'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  b</span><span>:</span><span> 'bar'</span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span> </span><span>a</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>foo</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  bar</span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ATTR</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ELEMENTS</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>Object</span><span>[</span><span>]</span><span> b</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> 'foo'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  b</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>[</span><span>'bar'</span><span>,</span><span> 'baz'</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>true</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span> </span><span>a</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>foo</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  bar</span> </span><span><span>  baz</span> </span><span><span>  123</span> </span><span><span>  true</span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

The [XmlFormat.MIXED](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#MIXED) format is similar to [XmlFormat.ELEMENTS](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#ELEMENTS) except elements names on primitive types (string/number/boolean/null) are stripped from the output.
This format particularly useful when combined with bean dictionaries to produce mixed content.
The bean dictionary isn't used during serializationbut it is needed during parsing to resolve bean types.
The [XmlFormat.MIXED\_PWS](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#MIXED_PWS) format identical to [XmlFormat.MIXED](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#MIXED) except whitespace characters are preserved in the output.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>Without annotations</th><th>With annotations</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>MIXED</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Beanp</span><span>(</span><span>dictionary</span><span>=</span><span>{</span><span>MyBeanX</span><span>.</span><span>class</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>MyBeanY</span><span>.</span><span>class</span><span>}</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>Object</span><span>[</span><span>]</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>@Bean</span><span>(</span><span>typeName</span><span>=</span><span>"X"</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBeanX</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ATTR</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> b</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>@Bean</span><span>(</span><span>typeName</span><span>=</span><span>"Y"</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBeanY</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>ATTR</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> c</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>[</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    'foo'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>{</span><span> _type</span><span>:</span><span>'X'</span><span>,</span><span> b</span><span>:</span><span>'bar' </span><span>}</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    'baz'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>{</span><span> _type</span><span>:</span><span>'Y'</span><span>,</span><span> c</span><span>:</span><span>'qux' </span><span>}</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    'quux'</span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>]</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>      </span><span>&lt;</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span>bar</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span>baz</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>      </span><span>&lt;</span><span>c</span><span>&gt;</span><span>qux</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>c</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span>quux</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>string</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  foo</span><span>&lt;</span><span>X</span><span> </span><span>b</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>bar</span><span>'</span><span>/&gt;</span><span>baz</span><span>&lt;</span><span>Y</span><span> </span><span>c</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>qux</span><span>'</span><span>/&gt;</span><span>quux</span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

Whitespace (tabs and newlines) are not added to MIXED child nodes in readable-output mode.
This helps ensures strings in the serialized output can be losslessly parsed back into their original forms when they
contain whitespace characters.
If the [XMLInputFactory.IS\_REPLACING\_ENTITY\_REFERENCES](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.xml/javax/xml/stream/XMLInputFactory.html#IS_REPLACING_ENTITY_REFERENCES) setting was not useless in Java, we could support lossless readable XML for MIXED content.
But as of Java 8, it still does not work.
XML suffers from other deficiencies as well that affect MIXED content.
For example, and are equivalent in XML and indistinguishable by the Java XML parsers.
This makes it impossible to differentiate between an empty element and an element containing an empty string.
This causes empty strings to get lost in translation.
To alleviate this, we use the constructs "*xE000*" to represent an empty string, and "*x0020*" to represent leading and
trailing spaces.
The examples below show how whitespace is handled under various circumstances:

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>XML</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>@Bean</span><span>(</span><span>typeName</span><span>=</span><span>"X"</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>TEXT</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a </span><span>=</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>X</span><span>/&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>@Bean</span><span>(</span><span>typeName</span><span>=</span><span>"X"</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>TEXT</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a </span><span>=</span><span> </span><span>""</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>X</span><span>&gt;</span><span>_xE000_</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>X</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>@Bean</span><span>(</span><span>typeName</span><span>=</span><span>"X"</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>TEXT</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span>[</span><span>]</span><span> a </span><span>=</span><span> </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>MIXED_PWS</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span>[</span><span>]</span><span> a </span><span>=</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span>"  "</span><span>}</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>X</span><span>&gt;</span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>X</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>@Bean</span><span>(</span><span>typeName</span><span>=</span><span>"X"</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>MIXED_PWS</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span>[</span><span>]</span><span> a </span><span>=</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span>"  foobar  "</span><span>}</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>X</span><span>&gt;</span><span>  foobar  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>X</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

It should be noted that when using MIXED, you are not guaranteed to parse back the exact same content since side-by-side
strings in the content will end up concatenated when parsed.

The [XmlFormat.TEXT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#TEXT) format is similar to [XmlFormat.MIXED](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#MIXED) except it's meant for solitary objects that get serialized as simple child text nodes.
Any object that can be serialize to a `String` can be used.
The [XmlFormat.TEXT\_PWS](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#TEXT_PWS) is the same except
whitespace is preserved in the output.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>Without annotations</th><th>With annotations</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>TEXT</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> 'foo'</span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>a</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  foo</span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

The [XmlFormat.XMLTEXT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#XMLTEXT) format is similar to [XmlFormat.TEXT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlFormat.html#TEXT) except it's meant for strings containing XML that should be serialized as-is to the document.
Any object that can be serialize to a `String` can be used.
During parsing, the element content gets parsed with the rest of the document and then re-serialized to XML before being
set as the property value.
This process may not be perfect (e.g.
double quotes may be replaced by single quotes, etc...).

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>With TEXT annotation</th><th>With XMLTEXT annotation</th></tr><tr><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>class</span><span> </span><span>MyBean</span><span> </span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>@Xml</span><span>(</span><span>format</span><span>=</span><span>XmlFormat</span><span>.</span><span>XMLTEXT</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>public</span><span> </span><span>String</span><span> a</span><span>;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  a</span><span>:</span><span> 'Some &lt;b&gt;XML&lt;/b&gt; text'</span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  Some </span><span>&amp;lt;</span><span>b</span><span>&amp;gt;</span><span>XML</span><span>&amp;lt;</span><span>/b</span><span>&amp;gt;</span><span> text</span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  Some </span><span>&lt;</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span>XML</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>b</span><span>&gt;</span><span> text</span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>object</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

---

<a id="topics-xmlnamespaces"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/XmlNamespaces/ -->

<!-- page_index: 70 -->

# Namespaces

Let's go back to the example of our original `Person` bean class but add some namespace annotations:

```java
@Xml(prefix="per") 
@Bean(typeName="person") 
public class Person { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String name; 
    @Swap(TemporalCalendarSwap.IsoInstant.class) public Calendar birthDate; 
    public List addresses; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
 
@Xml(prefix="addr") 
@Bean(typeName="address") 
public class Address { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    @Xml(prefix="mail") public String street, city; 
    @Xml(prefix="mail") public StateEnum state; 
    @Xml(prefix="mail") public int zip; 
    public boolean isCurrent; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
```

The namespace URLs can either be defined as part of the [@Xml](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/Xml.html)
annotation, or can be defined at the package level with the [@XmlSchema](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/annotation/XmlSchema.html) annotation.

Below shows it defined at the package level:

```java
@XmlSchema( 
    prefix="ab",  // Default namespace 
    xmlNs={ 
        @XmlNs(prefix="ab", namespaceURI="http://www.apache.org/addressBook/"), 
        @XmlNs(prefix="per", namespaceURI="http://www.apache.org/person/"), 
        @XmlNs(prefix="addr", namespaceURI="http://www.apache.org/address/"), 
        @XmlNs(prefix="mail", namespaceURI="http://www.apache.org/mail/") 
    } 
) 
package org.apache.juneau.examples.addressbook; 
```

```java
Person person = new Person() 
    .name("John Smith") 
    .birthDate("1946-08-12T00:00:00Z") 
    .addresses( 
        new Address() 
            .street("100 Main Street") 
            .city("Anywhereville") 
            .state(NY) 
            .zip(12345) 
            .isCurrent(true); 
    ); 
 
// Create a new serializer with readable output, this time with namespaces enabled. 
// Note that this is identical to XmlSerializer.DEFAULT_NS_SQ_READABLE. 
XmlSerializer serializer = XmlSerializer.create().ns().ws().sq().build(); 
 
String xml = serializer.serialize(p); 
```

Now when we run this code, we'll see namespaces added to our output:

```xml
<per:person> 
    <per:name>John Smith</per:name> 
    <per:birthDate>1946-08-12T04:00:00Z</per:birthDate> 
    <per:addresses> 
        <addr:address> 
            <mail:street>100 Main Street</mail:street> 
            <mail:city>Anywhereville</mail:city> 
            <mail:state>NY</mail:state> 
            <mail:zip>12345</mail:zip> 
            <addr:isCurrent>true</addr:isCurrent> 
        </addr:address> 
    </per:addresses> 
</per:person> 
```

Enabling the `XmlSerializer.XML_addNamespaceUrisToRootsetting` results in the namespace URLs being added to the root
node:

```xml
<per:person 
        xmlns='http://www.apache.org/2013/Juneau' 
        xmlns:per='http://www.apache.org/person/' 
        xmlns:addr='http://www.apache.org/address/' 
        xmlns:mail='http://www.apache.org/mail/' 
    > 
    <per:name>John Smith</per:name> 
    <per:birthDate>1946-08-12T04:00:00Z</per:birthDate> 
    <per:addresses> 
        <addr:address> 
            <mail:street>100 Main Street</mail:street> 
            <mail:city>Anywhereville</mail:city> 
            <mail:state>NY</mail:state> 
            <mail:zip>12345</mail:zip> 
            <addr:isCurrent>true</addr:isCurrent> 
        </addr:address> 
    </per:addresses> 
</per:person> 
```

We can simplify the output by setting the default namespace on the serializer so that all the elements do not need to be
prefixed:

```java
// Create a new serializer with readable output, this time with namespaces enabled. 
XmlSerializer serializer = XmlSerializer.create().ws().sq().ns() 
    .defaultNamespaceUri("http://www.apache.org/person/") 
 
```

This produces the following equivalent where the elements don't need prefixes since they're already in the default
document namespace:

```xml
<person 
        xmlns:juneau='http://www.apache.org/2013/Juneau' 
        xmlns='http://www.apache.org/person/' 
        xmlns:addr='http://www.apache.org/address/' 
        xmlns:mail='http://www.apache.org/mail/' 
    > 
    <name>John Smith</name> 
    <birthDate>1946-08-12T04:00:00Z</birthDate> 
    <addresses> 
        <addr:address> 
            <mail:street>100 Main Street</mail:street> 
            <mail:city>Anywhereville</mail:city> 
            <mail:state>NY</mail:state> 
            <mail:zip>12345</mail:zip> 
            <addr:isCurrent>true</addr:isCurrent> 
        </addr:address> 
    </addresses> 
</person> 
```

By default, the XML serializer class will make a first-pass over the data structure to look for namespaces defined on
classes and bean properties.
In high-performance environments, you may want to consider disabling auto-detection and providing your own explicit list
of namespaces to the serializer to avoid this scanning step using [XmlSerializer.Builder.disableAutoDetectNamespaces()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.Builder.html#disableAutoDetectNamespaces()).

The following code will produce the same output as before but will perform slightly better since it avoids this pre-scan
step.

```java
// Create a new serializer with readable output, this time with namespaces enabled. 
XmlSerializer serializer = XmlSerializer.create() 
    .ws() 
    .sq() 
    .autoDetectNamespaces(false) 
    .namespaces("{per:'http://www.apache.org/person/'}") 
    .build(); 
```

---

<a id="topics-htmlbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 71 -->

# HTML Basics

Juneau supports converting arbitrary POJOs to and from HTML.
Built on top of the existing XML parser, it also uses a STaX parser and creates POJOs directly without intermediate DOM
objects.

The primary use case for HTML serialization is rendering POJOs in easy-to-read format in REST interfaces.

---

<a id="topics-htmlmethodology"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlMethodology/ -->

<!-- page_index: 72 -->

# HTML Methodology

The following examples show how different data types are represented in HTML.
They mirror how the data structures are represented in JSON.

The representation for simple types mirror those produced by the XML serializer.
Tags are added to help differentiate data types when they cannot be inferred through reflection.
These tags are ignored by browsers and treated as plain text.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>HTML</th></tr><tr><td>string</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>'foo'</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>foo</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>boolean</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>true</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span><span>true</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>integer</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>123</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>float</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>1.23</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>1.23</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>null</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>null</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

`Maps` and beans are represented as tables.
The `_type` attribute is added to differentiate between objects (`Maps`/beans) and arrays (arrays/`Collections`).

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>HTML</th></tr><tr><td>Map</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k1</span><span>:</span><span> 'v1'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k2</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>table</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>object</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k1</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>v1</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k2</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>table</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Map</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k1</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k2</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k3</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>table</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>object</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k1</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k2</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>1.23</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k3</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>table</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Map</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>{</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k1</span><span>:</span><span> 'v1'</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k2</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k3</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k4</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>true</span><span>,</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  k5</span><span>:</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>}</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>table</span><span> </span><span>_type</span><span>=</span><span>'</span><span>object</span><span>'</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k1</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>v1</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k2</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k3</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>1.23</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k4</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span>k5</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>td</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>tr</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>table</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

`Collections` and arrays are represented as ordered lists.

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>HTML</th></tr><tr><td>String[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>'foo'</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Number[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span><span><span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Object[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>'foo'</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>true</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span><span><span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>String[][]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>[</span><span>'foo'</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span> </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>    </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>int[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>123</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>boolean[]</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>true</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span><span>true</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>boolean</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Data type</th><th>JSON example</th><th>HTML</th></tr><tr><td>List&lt;String&gt;</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>'foo'</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>foo</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>null</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span></span><span>&lt;/</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>List&lt;Number&gt;</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>[</span><span>123</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>1.23</span><span>,</span><span> </span><span>null</span><span>]</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>&lt;</span><span>ul</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>123</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>  </span><span>&lt;</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>1.23</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>number</span><span>&gt;</span><span>&lt;/</span><span>li</span><span>&gt;</span><span></span> </span><span><span>
