<a id="topics-htmlserializers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlSerializers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 73 -->

# HTML Serializers

The [HtmlSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlSerializer.html) class is used to serialize POJOs into
HTML.
The [HtmlDocSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.html) class is the same but wraps the
serialized POJO inside a document template consisting of header, nav, aside, and footer sections.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this serializer is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[BeanTraverseContext.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanTraverseContext.Builder.html)
[Serializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/Serializer.Builder.html)
[WriterSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/WriterSerializer.Builder.html)
[XmlSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.Builder.html)
[HtmlSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlSerializer.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured serializers are provided for convenience:

[HtmlSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlSerializer.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT)
[DEFAULT\_SQ](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT_SQ)
[DEFAULT\_SQ\_READABLE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlSerializer.html#DEFAULT_SQ_READABLE)
[HtmlDocSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.html#DEFAULT)

---

<a id="topics-htmlparsers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlParsers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 74 -->

# HTML Parsers

The [HtmlParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlParser.html) class is used to parse HTML into POJOs.
They can also parse the contents produced by [HtmlDocSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.html).

The class hierarchy for the builder of this parser is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[Parser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/Parser.Builder.html)
[ReaderParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/ReaderParser.Builder.html)
[HtmlParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlParser.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured parsers are provided for convenience:

[HtmlParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlParser.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlParser.html#DEFAULT)

---

<a id="topics-htmlannotation"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlAnnotation/ -->

<!-- page_index: 75 -->

# @Html Annotation

The [@Html](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html) annotation can be used to customize how POJOs are
serialized to HTML on a per-class/field/method basis.

[Html](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html)
[anchorText](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#anchorText()) [format](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#format()) [link](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#link()) [noTableHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#noTableHeaders()) [noTables](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#noTables()) [render](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#render())

The [@Html(link)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#link()) annotation adds a hyperlink to a bean
property when rendered as HTML.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> public class FileSpace {
>     // Add a hyperlink to this bean property.
>     @Html(link="servlet:/drive/{drive}")
>     public String getDrive() {...}
> }
> ```

The [@Html(anchorText)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#anchorText()) annotation is used to
specify the anchor text of a hyperlink.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Produces CLICK ME! when serialized to HTML.
> public class FileSpace {
>     // Add a hyperlink to this bean property.
>     @Html(link="servlet:/drive/{drive}", anchorText="CLICK ME!")
>     public String getDrive() {...}
> }
> ```

The [@Html(format)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#format()) annotation is used to specify what
format to use for HTML elements.
For example, the HTML beans defined in the [html5](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/bean/html5/package-summary.html) package use
`format=XML` so that the beans get serialized as standard XML:

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Parent class of all HTML DTO bean classes.
> @Html(format=XML)
> public abstract class HtmlElement {...}
> ```

The [@Html(noTableHeaders)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#noTableHeaders()) annotation is used
to prevent beans from being serialized with table headers.

The [@Html(noTables)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#noTables()) annotation is used to force
beans to be serialized as trees instead of tables

---

<a id="topics-htmlrenderannotation"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlRenderAnnotation/ -->

<!-- page_index: 76 -->

# @Html(render) Annotation

The [@Html(render)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/annotation/Html.html#render()) annotation allows for custom
rendering of bean property values when serialized as HTML.
Using this class, you can alter the CSS style and HTML content of the bean property.

The following example shows two render classes that customize the appearance of the `pctFull` and `status` columns in
the code below:

```java
import static org.apache.juneau.bean.html5.HtmlBuilder.*; 
 
// Our bean class 
public class FileSpace { 
 
    private final String drive; 
    private final long total, available; 
 
    public FileSpace(String drive, long total, long available) { 
        this.drive = drive; 
        this.total = total; 
        this.available = available; 
    } 
 
    @Html(link="drive/{drive}") 
    public String getDrive() { 
        return drive; 
    } 
 
    public long getTotal() { 
        return total; 
    } 
 
    public long getAvailable() { 
        return available; 
    } 
 
    @Html(render=FileSpacePctRender.class) 
    public float getPctFull() { 
        return ((100 * available) / total); 
    } 
 
    @Html(render=FileSpaceStatusRender.class) 
    public FileSpaceStatus getStatus() { 
        float pf = getPctFull(); 
        if (pf OK; 
        if (pf WARNING; 
        return FileSpaceStatus.SEVERE; 
    } 
} 
```

```java
// Possible values for the getStatus() method 
public enum FileSpaceStatus { 
    OK, WARNING, SEVERE; 
} 
```

```java
// Custom render for getPctFull() method 
public class FileSpacePctRender extends HtmlRender { 
 
    @Override 
    public String getStyle(SerializerSession session, Float value) { 
        if (value = 90) 
            return div( 
                String.format("%.0f%%", value), 
                style("@keyframes color_change { from { background-color: red; } to { background-color: yellow; }") 
            ); 
        return String.format("%.0f%%", value); 
    } 
} 
```

```java
// Custom render for getStatus() method 
public class FileSpaceStatusRender extends HtmlRender { 
 
    @Override 
    public String getStyle(SerializerSession session, FileSpaceStatus value) { 
        return "text-align:center"; 
    } 
 
    @Override 
    public Object getContent(SerializerSession session, FileSpaceStatus value) { 
        switch (value) { 
            case OK:  return img().src(URI.create("servlet:/htdocs/ok.png")); 
            case WARNING:  return img().src(URI.create("servlet:/htdocs/warning.png")); 
            default: return img().src(URI.create("servlet:/htdocs/severe.png")); 
        } 
    } 
} 
```

---

<a id="topics-htmldocserializer"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlDocSerializer/ -->

<!-- page_index: 77 -->

# HtmlDocSerializer

[HtmlDocSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.html) is an extension of [HtmlSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlSerializer.html) that wraps serialized POJOs in a complete HTML document.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this serializer is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[BeanTraverseContext.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanTraverseContext.Builder.html)
[Serializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/Serializer.Builder.html)
[WriterSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/WriterSerializer.Builder.html)
[XmlSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/xml/XmlSerializer.Builder.html)
[HtmlSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlSerializer.Builder.html)
[HtmlStrippedDocSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlStrippedDocSerializer.Builder.html)
[HtmlDocSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

This class is used extensively in the creation of POJO-based user interfaces in the REST API.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> /**
> * Sample REST resource that prints out a simple "Hello world!" message.
> */
> @Rest(path="/helloWorld")
> @HtmlDocConfig(
>     navlinks={
>         "up: request:/..",
>         "options: servlet:/?method=OPTIONS"
>     },
>     aside={
>         "",
>         "   <p>This page shows a resource that simply response with a 'Hello world!' message</p>",
>         "   <p>The POJO serialized is a simple String.</p>",
>         ""
>     }
> )
> public class HelloWorldResource extends BasicRestServlet {
>     // ...
> }
> ```

The [HtmlDocSerializer.Builder.template(Class)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.Builder.html#template(java.lang.Class))
setting defines a template for the HTML page being generated.
The default template is described next.

---

<a id="topics-basichtmldoctemplate"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/BasicHtmlDocTemplate/ -->

<!-- page_index: 78 -->

# BasicHtmlDocTemplate

The [BasicHtmlDocTemplate](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/BasicHtmlDocTemplate.html) class defines a default
template for HTML documents created by [HtmlDocSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.html).

The HTML document created by this template consists of the following structure:

```xml
<html> 
    <head> 
        <style type='text/css'> 
            CSS styles and links to stylesheets 
        </style> 
    </head> 
    <body> 
        <header> 
            Page header 
        </header> 
        <nav> 
            Navigation links 
        </nav> 
        <aside> 
            Side-bar text 
        </aside> 
        <article> 
            Contents of serialized object 
        </article> 
        <footer> 
            Footer message 
        </footer> 
    </body> 
</html> 
```

---

<a id="topics-htmlcustomtemplates"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlCustomTemplates/ -->

<!-- page_index: 79 -->

# Custom Templates

Custom page templates can be created by implementing the [HtmlDocTemplate](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocTemplate.html) interface and associating it with your [HtmlDocSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.html) using the [HtmlDocSerializer.Builder.template(Class)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocSerializer.Builder.html#template(java.lang.Class)) setting.

[HtmlDocTemplate](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocTemplate.html)
[writeTo(HtmlDocSerializerSession,HtmlWriter,Object)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlDocTemplate.html#writeTo(org.apache.juneau.html.HtmlDocSerializerSession,org.apache.juneau.html.HtmlWriter,java.lang.Object))

The interface implementation is open-ended allowing you to define the contents of the page any way you wish.

---

<a id="topics-htmlschemasupport"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlSchemaSupport/ -->

<!-- page_index: 80 -->

# HTML-Schema Support

The [HtmlSchemaSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/html/HtmlSchemaSerializer.html) class is the HTML-equivalent
to the [JsonSchemaSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSchemaSerializer.html) class.
It's used to generate HTML versions of JSON-Schema documents that describe the output generated by the [JsonSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/json/JsonSerializer.html) class.

```java
public class Person { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String name; 
    public Calendar birthDate; 
    public List addresses; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
 
public class Address { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String street, city; 
    public StateEnum state; 
    public int zip; 
    public boolean isCurrent; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
```

The code for creating our POJO model and generating HTML-Schema is shown below:

```java
// Get the one of the default schema serializers. 
HtmlSchemaSerializer serializer = HtmlSchemaSerializer.DEFAULT_SIMPLE_READABLE; 
 
// Get the HTML Schema for the POJO. 
String htmlSchema = serializer.serialize(new Person()); 
 
// This also works. 
htmlSchema = serializer.serialize(Person.class); 
```

The result is the HTML table shown below:

<table class="bordered unstyled w800"><tr><td>type</td><td>object</td></tr><tr><td>properties</td><td><table><tr><td>name</td><td><table><tr><td>type</td><td>string</td></tr></table></td></tr><tr><td>birthDate</td><td><table><tr><td>type</td><td>string</td></tr></table></td></tr><tr><td>addresses</td><td><table><tr><td>type</td><td>array</td></tr><tr><td>items</td><td><table><tr><td>type</td><td>object</td></tr><tr><td>properties</td><td><table><tr><td>street</td><td><table><tr><td>type</td><td>string</td></tr></table></td></tr><tr><td>city</td><td><table><tr><td>type</td><td>string</td></tr></table></td></tr><tr><td>state</td><td><table><tr><td>type</td><td>string</td></tr><tr><td>enum</td><td><ul><li>AL</li><li>PA</li><li>NC</li></ul></td></tr></table></td></tr><tr><td>zip</td><td><table><tr><td>type</td><td>integer</td></tr><tr><td>format</td><td>int32</td></tr></table></td></tr><tr><td>isCurrent</td><td><table><tr><td>type</td><td>boolean</td></tr></table></td></tr></table></td></tr></table></td></tr></table></td></tr></table></td></tr></table>

---

<a id="topics-uonbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/UonBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 81 -->

# UON Basics

Juneau supports converting arbitrary POJOs to and from UON strings using ultra-efficient serializers and parsers.

The serializer converts POJOs directly to UON strings without the need for intermediate DOM objects using a
highly-efficient state machine.
Likewise, the parser creates POJOs directly from UON strings without the need for intermediate DOM objects.

Juneau uses UON (URL-Encoded Object Notation) for representing POJOs.

The UON specification can be found [here](assets/files/rfc-uon-1a8be4ca5ddd8c92b5c9ea2dfd462c39_0b52170d19542721.txt).

The following example shows JSON for a typical bean:

```java
public class Person { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String name; 
    @Swap(TemporalCalendarSwap.IsoInstant.class) public Calendar birthDate; 
    public List addresses; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
 
public class Address { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String street, city; 
    public StateEnum state; 
    public int zip; 
    public boolean isCurrent; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
```

```java
Person person = new Person() 
    .name("John Smith") 
    .birthDate("1946-08-12T00:00:00Z") 
    .addresses( 
        new Address() 
            .street("100 Main Street") 
            .city("Anywhereville") 
            .state(NY) 
            .zip(12345) 
            .isCurrent(true); 
    ); 
```

```javascript
( 
    name='John+Smith', 
    birthDate='1946-08-12T00:00:00Z', 
    addresses=@( 
        ( 
            street='100 Main Street', 
            city=Anywhereville, 
            state=NY, 
            zip=12345, 
            isCurrent=true 
        ) 
    ) 
) 
```

---

<a id="topics-uonmethodology"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/UonMethodology/ -->

<!-- page_index: 82 -->

# UON Methodology

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Java type</th><th>JSON equivalent</th><th>UON</th></tr><tr><td><code>Maps</code>/beans</td><td>OBJECT</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>(</span><span>b1</span><span>=</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>b2</span><span>=</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>(</span><span>b1</span><span>=</span><span>(</span><span>c1</span><span>=</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>c2</span><span>=</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span>)</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><code>Collections</code>/arrays</td><td>ARRAY</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span>,</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>x3</span><span>,</span><span>x4</span><span>)</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>(</span><span>b1</span><span>=</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>b2</span><span>=</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span>,</span><span>(</span><span>c1</span><span>=</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>c2</span><span>=</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span>)</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Booleans</td><td>BOOLEAN</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>true</span><span>&amp;</span><span>a2</span><span>=</span><span>false</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>int/float/double/...</td><td>NUMBER</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>123</span><span>&amp;</span><span>a2</span><span>=</span><span>1.23e1</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>null</td><td>NULL</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>null</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>String</td><td>STRING</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>foobar</span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>'true'</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>'null'</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>'123'</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>' string </span><span>with</span><span> </span><span>whitespace</span><span> '</span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>'string </span><span>with</span><span> </span><span>~</span><span>'escaped</span><span>~</span><span>' quotes'</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

Refer to the [UON specification](assets/files/rfc-uon-1a8be4ca5ddd8c92b5c9ea2dfd462c39_0b52170d19542721.txt) for a complete set of syntax rules.

---

<a id="topics-uonserializers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/UonSerializers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 83 -->

# UON Serializers

The [UonSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonSerializer.html) class is used to serialize POJOs into UON.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this serializer is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[BeanTraverseContext.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanTraverseContext.Builder.html)
[Serializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/Serializer.Builder.html)
[WriterSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/WriterSerializer.Builder.html)
[UonSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonSerializer.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured serializers are provided for convenience:

[UonSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonSerializer.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonSerializer.html#DEFAULT)
[DEFAULT\_ENCODING](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonSerializer.html#DEFAULT_ENCODING)
[DEFAULT\_READABLE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonSerializer.html#DEFAULT_READABLE)

---

<a id="topics-uonparsers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/UonParsers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 84 -->

# UON Parsers

The [UonParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonParser.html) class is used to parse UON into POJOs.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this parser is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[Parser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/Parser.Builder.html)
[ReaderParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/ReaderParser.Builder.html)
[UonParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonParser.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured parsers are provided for convenience:

[UonParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonParser.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonParser.html#DEFAULT)
[DEFAULT\_DECODING](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonParser.html#DEFAULT_DECODING)

---

<a id="topics-urlencodingbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/UrlEncodingBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 85 -->

# URL-Encoding Basics

Juneau supports converting arbitrary POJOs to and from URL-encoded strings using ultra-efficient serializers and
parsers.

The serializer converts POJOs directly to URL-encoded strings without the need for intermediate DOM objects using a
highly-efficient state machine.
Likewise, the parser creates POJOs directly from URL-encoded strings without the need for intermediate DOM objects.

Juneau uses UON (URL-Encoded Object Notation) for representing POJOs as URL-Encoded values in key-value pairs.

The UON specification can be found [here](assets/files/rfc-uon-1a8be4ca5ddd8c92b5c9ea2dfd462c39_0b52170d19542721.txt).

The following example shows JSON for a typical bean:

```java
public class Person { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String name; 
    @Swap(TemporalCalendarSwap.IsoInstant.class) public Calendar birthDate; 
    public List addresses; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
 
public class Address { 
 
    // Bean properties 
    public String street, city; 
    public StateEnum state; 
    public int zip; 
    public boolean isCurrent; 
 
    // Getters/setters omitted 
} 
```

```java
Person person = new Person() 
    .name("John Smith") 
    .birthDate("1946-08-12T00:00:00Z") 
    .addresses( 
        new Address() 
            .street("100 Main Street") 
            .city("Anywhereville") 
            .state(NY) 
            .zip(12345) 
            .isCurrent(true); 
    ); 
```

```text
name='John+Smith' 
&birthDate='1946-08-12T00:00:00Z' 
&addresses=@( 
    ( 
        street='100 Main Street', 
        city=Anywhereville, 
        state=NY, 
        zip=12345, 
        isCurrent=true 
    ) 
) 
```

---

<a id="topics-urlencmethodology"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/UrlEncMethodology/ -->

<!-- page_index: 86 -->

# URL-Encoding Methodology

<table class="code-table"><tr><th>Java type</th><th>JSON equivalent</th><th>UON</th></tr><tr><td><code>Maps</code>/beans</td><td>OBJECT</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>(</span><span>b1</span><span>=</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>b2</span><span>=</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>(</span><span>b1</span><span>=</span><span>(</span><span>c1</span><span>=</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>c2</span><span>=</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span>)</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><code>Collections</code>/arrays</td><td>ARRAY</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span>,</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>x3</span><span>,</span><span>x4</span><span>)</span><span>)</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>@</span><span>(</span><span>(</span><span>b1</span><span>=</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>b2</span><span>=</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span>,</span><span>(</span><span>c1</span><span>=</span><span>x1</span><span>,</span><span>c2</span><span>=</span><span>x2</span><span>)</span><span>)</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>Booleans</td><td>BOOLEAN</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>true</span><span>&amp;</span><span>a2</span><span>=</span><span>false</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>int/float/double/...</td><td>NUMBER</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>123</span><span>&amp;</span><span>a2</span><span>=</span><span>1.23e1</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>null</td><td>NULL</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>null</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr><tr><td>String</td><td>STRING</td><td><div><div><pre><code><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>foobar</span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>'true'</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>'null'</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>'123'</span><span></span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>' string </span><span>with</span><span> </span><span>whitespace</span><span> '</span> </span><span><span>a1</span><span>=</span><span>'string </span><span>with</span><span> </span><span>~</span><span>'escaped</span><span>~</span><span>' quotes'</span> </span></code></pre></div></div></td></tr></table>

Refer to the [UON specification](assets/files/rfc-uon-1a8be4ca5ddd8c92b5c9ea2dfd462c39_0b52170d19542721.txt) for a complete set of syntax rules.

---

<a id="topics-urlencserializers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/UrlEncSerializers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 87 -->

# URL-Encoding Serializers

The [UrlEncodingSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingSerializer.html) class is used to
serialize POJOs into URL-Encoding.
The class hierarchy for the builder of this serializer is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[BeanTraverseContext.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanTraverseContext.Builder.html)
[Serializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/Serializer.Builder.html)
[WriterSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/WriterSerializer.Builder.html)
[UonSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonSerializer.Builder.html)
[UrlEncodingSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingSerializer.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.
The following pre-configured serializers are provided for convenience:

[UrlEncodingSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingSerializer.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingSerializer.html#DEFAULT)
[DEFAULT\_EXPANDED](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingSerializer.html#DEFAULT_EXPANDED)
[DEFAULT\_PLAINTEXT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingSerializer.html#DEFAULT_PLAINTEXT)
[DEFAULT\_READABLE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingSerializer.html#DEFAULT_READABLE)

---

<a id="topics-urlencparsers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/UrlEncParsers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 88 -->

# URL-Encoding Parsers

The [UrlEncodingParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingParser.html) class is used to parse
URL-Encoding into POJOs.
The class hierarchy for the builder of this parser is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[Parser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/Parser.Builder.html)
[ReaderParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/ReaderParser.Builder.html)
[UonParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/uon/UonParser.Builder.html)
[UrlEncodingParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingParser.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.
The following pre-configured parsers are provided for convenience:

[UrlEncodingParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingParser.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingParser.html#DEFAULT)

---

<a id="topics-urlencodingannotation"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/UrlEncodingAnnotation/ -->

<!-- page_index: 89 -->

# @UrlEncoding Annotation

The [@UrlEncoding](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/annotation/UrlEncoding.html) annotation is used to
override the behavior of [UrlEncodingSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/UrlEncodingSerializer.html)
on individual bean classes or properties.

[UrlEncoding](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/annotation/UrlEncoding.html)
[expandedParams](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/annotation/UrlEncoding.html#expandedParams())

The [expandedParams](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/urlencoding/annotation/UrlEncoding.html#expandedParams()) setting is
used to force bean properties of type array or `Collection` to be expanded into multiple key/value pairings.

---

<a id="topics-messagepackbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MessagePackBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 90 -->

# MessagePack Basics

Juneau supports converting arbitrary POJOs to and from MessagePack using ultra-efficient serializers and parsers.
MessagePack is a compact binary form of JSON.

The serialization support for MessagePack mirrors that of JSON.

---

<a id="topics-msgpackserializers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MsgPackSerializers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 91 -->

# MessagePack Serializers

The [MsgPackSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/msgpack/MsgPackSerializer.html) class is used to serialize POJOs
into MessagePack.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this serializer is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[BeanTraverseContext.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanTraverseContext.Builder.html)
[Serializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/Serializer.Builder.html)
[OutputStreamSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/OutputStreamSerializer.Builder.html)
[MsgPackSerializer.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/msgpack/MsgPackSerializer.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured serializers are provided for convenience:

[MsgPackSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/msgpack/MsgPackSerializer.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/msgpack/MsgPackSerializer.html#DEFAULT)

---

<a id="topics-msgpackparsers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/MsgPackParsers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 92 -->

# MessagePack Parsers

The [MsgPackParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/msgpack/MsgPackParser.html) class is used to parse MessagePack into
POJOs.

The class hierarchy for the builder of this parser is:

[Context.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/Context.Builder.html)
[BeanContextable.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContextable.Builder.html)
[Parser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/Parser.Builder.html)
[InputStreamParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/InputStreamParser.Builder.html)
[MsgPackParser.Builder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/msgpack/MsgPackParser.Builder.html)

Refer to the builder javadocs for configurable settings.

The following pre-configured parsers are provided for convenience:

[MsgPackParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/msgpack/MsgPackParser.html)
[DEFAULT](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/msgpack/MsgPackParser.html#DEFAULT)
