<a id="topics-listifiers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Listifiers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 146 -->

# Listifiers

Listifiers convert collection-like objects into lists for use in BCT assertions. They provide a unified way to work with various collection types, iterators, streams, and other iterable data structures.

BCT comes with comprehensive built-in listifiers for collection-like Java types:

```java
// Collection types 
List<String> list = List.of("a", "b", "c"); 
assertList(list, "a", "b", "c");  // Uses collectionListifier() 
 
Set<Integer> set = Set.of(1, 2, 3); 
assertList(set, "1", "2", "3");  // Uses iterableListifier() 
 
// Iterator types 
Iterator<String> iterator = List.of("x", "y", "z").iterator(); 
assertList(iterator, "x", "y", "z");  // Uses iteratorListifier() 
 
// Stream types 
Stream<String> stream = Stream.of("p", "q", "r"); 
assertList(stream, "p", "q", "r");  // Uses streamListifier() 
 
// Enumeration types 
Vector<String> vector = new Vector<>(List.of("m", "n", "o")); 
Enumeration<String> enumeration = vector.elements(); 
assertList(enumeration, "m", "n", "o");  // Uses enumerationListifier() 
 
// Map types 
Map<String, String> map = Map.of("key1", "value1", "key2", "value2"); 
assertList(map, "key1=value1", "key2=value2");  // Uses mapListifier() 
```

- `collectionListifier()` - Converts Collection objects to `List&lt;Object&gt;`
- `iterableListifier()` - Converts Iterable objects to `List&lt;Object&gt;`
- `iteratorListifier()` - Converts Iterator objects to `List&lt;Object&gt;` (consumes the iterator)
- `streamListifier()` - Converts Stream objects to `List&lt;Object&gt;` (terminates the stream)
- `enumerationListifier()` - Converts Enumeration objects to `List&lt;Object&gt;` (consumes the enumeration)
- `mapListifier()` - Converts Map objects to `List&lt;Map.Entry&gt;` objects

Define custom listifiers for domain-specific collection types:

```java
// Custom collection type 
Listifier<CustomCollection> customListifier = (conv, collection) -> { 
    if (collection == null) return null; 
    List<Object> result = new ArrayList<>(); 
    collection.forEach(result::add); 
    return result; 
}; 
 
// Registration 
var converter = BasicBeanConverter.builder() 
    .defaultSettings() 
    .addListifier(CustomCollection.class, customListifier) 
    .build(); 
```

```java
// Paginated result listifier 
Listifier<PaginatedResult> paginatedListifier = (conv, page) -> { 
    if (page == null) return null; 
    // Extract items from paginated wrapper 
    return new ArrayList<>(page.getItems()); 
}; 
 
// Database result set listifier 
Listifier<ResultSet> resultSetListifier = (conv, rs) -> { 
    if (rs == null) return null; 
    List<Object> rows = new ArrayList<>(); 
    try { 
        int columnCount = rs.getMetaData().getColumnCount(); 
        while (rs.next()) { 
            Map<String, Object> row = new LinkedHashMap<>(); 
            for (int i = 1; i <= columnCount; i++) { 
                row.put(rs.getMetaData().getColumnName(i), rs.getObject(i)); 
            } 
            rows.add(row); 
        } 
    } catch (SQLException e) { 
        throw new RuntimeException(e); 
    } 
    return rows; 
}; 
 
// Registration 
var converter = BasicBeanConverter.builder() 
    .defaultSettings() 
    .addListifier(PaginatedResult.class, paginatedListifier) 
    .addListifier(ResultSet.class, resultSetListifier) 
    .build(); 
```

```java
// Convert tree to flat list (breadth-first) 
Listifier<TreeNode> treeBreadthFirstListifier = (conv, root) -> { 
    if (root == null) return null; 
    List<Object> result = new ArrayList<>(); 
    Queue<TreeNode> queue = new LinkedList<>(); 
    queue.add(root); 
     
    while (!queue.isEmpty()) { 
        TreeNode node = queue.poll(); 
        result.add(node.getValue()); 
        queue.addAll(node.getChildren()); 
    } 
     
    return result; 
}; 
 
// Convert tree to flat list (depth-first) 
Listifier<TreeNode> treeDepthFirstListifier = (conv, root) -> { 
    if (root == null) return null; 
    List<Object> result = new ArrayList<>(); 
    collectDepthFirst(root, result); 
    return result; 
}; 
 
private void collectDepthFirst(TreeNode node, List<Object> result) { 
    if (node != null) { 
        result.add(node.getValue()); 
        node.getChildren().forEach(child -> collectDepthFirst(child, result)); 
    } 
} 
```

```java
// Test paginated results 
PaginatedResult<User> page = userService.getUsers(pageNumber); 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertList(page, "Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
 
// Test database results 
ResultSet rs = statement.executeQuery("SELECT name FROM users"); 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertList(rs,  
          predicate(row -> ((Map)row).get("name").equals("Alice")), 
          predicate(row -> ((Map)row).get("name").equals("Bob"))); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
```

```java
// Test collection properties 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertBean(paginatedResult,  
          "items{#{name}},totalCount",  
          "[{Alice},{Bob},{Charlie}],3"); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
```

Some listifiers consume their input:

```java
// Iterator is consumed - can only be used once 
Iterator<String> iterator = list.iterator(); 
assertList(iterator, "a", "b", "c"); 
// iterator is now exhausted and cannot be reused 
 
// Stream is terminated - can only be used once 
Stream<String> stream = Stream.of("a", "b", "c"); 
assertList(stream, "a", "b", "c"); 
// stream is now closed and cannot be reused 
 
// Enumeration is consumed - can only be used once 
Enumeration<String> enumeration = vector.elements(); 
assertList(enumeration, "a", "b", "c"); 
// enumeration is now exhausted 
```

```java
// Ensure thread-safe listifiers for concurrent testing 
Listifier<ThreadSafeCollection> threadSafeListifier = (conv, collection) -> { 
    if (collection == null) return null; 
    synchronized (collection) { 
        return new ArrayList<>(collection.getItems()); 
    } 
}; 
```

- Custom collection implementations
- Wrapper objects containing collections
- Database result sets or cursors
- Paginated results
- Tree or graph structures that need flattening
- Legacy collection types

- Always handle null input appropriately
- Return null for null input (not empty list)
- Create defensive copies when necessary
- Consider whether the listifier consumes its input
- Document consumption behavior clearly
- Ensure thread safety for shared listifiers

- Avoid copying large collections unnecessarily
- Use streaming approaches for large datasets
- Consider lazy evaluation for expensive conversions
- Be aware of memory implications for large lists

```java
// Robust listifier with error handling 
Listifier<DataSource> safeListifier = (conv, source) -> { 
    if (source == null) return null; 
    try { 
        return source.fetchAll(); 
    } catch (Exception e) { 
        // Return list with error indication 
        return List.of("Error: " + e.getMessage()); 
    } 
}; 
```

  - [Stringifiers](#topics-stringifiers) - Converting objects to strings
  - [Swappers](#topics-swappers) - Transforming objects before processing
- [PropertyExtractors](#topics-propertyextractors) - Custom property access logic

<a id="topics-swappers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Swappers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 147 -->

# Swappers

Swappers transform objects before they are processed by BCT assertions. They provide a way to unwrap, evaluate, or transform objects into more testable forms. Swappers are particularly useful for wrapper types like Optional, Supplier, and Future.

BCT comes with built-in swappers for common wrapper and lazy-evaluation types:

```java
// Optional types 
Optional<String> optional = Optional.of("Hello"); 
assertString("Hello", optional);  // Uses optionalSwapper() - unwraps to "Hello" 
 
Optional<String> emptyOptional = Optional.empty(); 
assertString("&lt;null&gt;", emptyOptional);  // Uses optionalSwapper() - unwraps to null 
 
// Supplier types 
Supplier<String> supplier = () -> "World"; 
assertString("World", supplier);  // Uses supplierSwapper() - calls get() to get "World" 
 
Supplier<String> expensiveSupplier = () -> { 
    // Simulate expensive computation 
    return "Expensive Result"; 
}; 
assertString("Expensive Result", expensiveSupplier);  // Uses supplierSwapper() - calls get() 
 
// Future types 
CompletableFuture<String> completedFuture = CompletableFuture.completedFuture("Done"); 
assertString("Done", completedFuture);  // Uses futureSwapper() - gets completed result 
 
CompletableFuture<String> pendingFuture = new CompletableFuture<>(); 
assertString("&lt;pending&gt;", pendingFuture);  // Uses futureSwapper() - shows pending status 
```

- `optionalSwapper()` - Unwraps Optional values to their contained value or null
- `supplierSwapper()` - Calls Supplier.get() to evaluate lazy values
- `futureSwapper()` - Extracts completed Future results or shows "<pending>" for incomplete futures

Define custom swappers for your domain-specific wrapper types:

```java
// Lazy wrapper swapper 
Swapper<LazyValue> lazySwapper = (conv, lazy) -> { 
    if (lazy == null) return null; 
    return lazy.getValue();  // Evaluate the lazy value 
}; 
 
// Result wrapper swapper 
Swapper<Result> resultSwapper = (conv, result) -> { 
    if (result == null) return null; 
    if (result.isSuccess()) { 
        return result.getValue(); 
    } else { 
        return "Error: " + result.getError(); 
    } 
}; 
 
// Registration 
var converter = BasicBeanConverter.builder() 
    .defaultSettings() 
    .addSwapper(LazyValue.class, lazySwapper) 
    .addSwapper(Result.class, resultSwapper) 
    .build(); 
```

```java
// Either/Union type swapper 
Swapper<Either<String, Integer>> eitherSwapper = (conv, either) -> { 
    if (either == null) return null; 
    return either.isLeft() ? either.getLeft() : either.getRight(); 
}; 
 
// Validation result swapper 
Swapper<ValidationResult> validationSwapper = (conv, validation) -> { 
    if (validation == null) return null; 
    if (validation.isValid()) { 
        return validation.getValue(); 
    } else { 
        // Return list of error messages 
        return validation.getErrors(); 
    } 
}; 
 
// Proxy object swapper 
Swapper<ProxyObject> proxySwapper = (conv, proxy) -> { 
    if (proxy == null) return null; 
    // Unwrap the proxy to get the real object 
    return proxy.getTarget(); 
}; 
 
// Registration 
var converter = BasicBeanConverter.builder() 
    .defaultSettings() 
    .addSwapper(Either.class, eitherSwapper) 
    .addSwapper(ValidationResult.class, validationSwapper) 
    .addSwapper(ProxyObject.class, proxySwapper) 
    .build(); 
```

```java
// Swap based on object state 
Swapper<CachedValue> cachedValueSwapper = (conv, cached) -> { 
    if (cached == null) return null; 
     
    if (cached.isExpired()) { 
        return "&lt;expired&gt;"; 
    } else if (cached.isPending()) { 
        return "&lt;pending&gt;"; 
    } else { 
        return cached.getValue(); 
    } 
}; 
 
// Usage 
assertString("myValue", cachedValue);  // "myValue" if valid 
assertString("&lt;expired&gt;", expiredValue);  // "&lt;expired&gt;" if expired 
```

```java
// Swapper that performs multiple transformations 
Swapper<ComplexWrapper> complexSwapper = (conv, wrapper) -> { 
    if (wrapper == null) return null; 
     
    // Step 1: Extract inner value 
    Object inner = wrapper.getInner(); 
     
    // Step 2: Apply transformation 
    if (inner instanceof Optional) { 
        inner = ((Optional<?>) inner).orElse(null); 
    } 
     
    // Step 3: Apply conversion 
    if (inner instanceof Supplier) { 
        inner = ((Supplier<?>) inner).get(); 
    } 
     
    return inner; 
}; 
```

```java
// Test Optional values 
Optional<User> optUser = userService.findById(123); 
assertBean(optUser, "name,email", "John,john@example.com"); 
 
// Test Supplier values 
Supplier<Config> configSupplier = () -> loadConfig(); 
assertBean(configSupplier, "timeout,retries", "30000,3"); 
 
// Test Future values 
CompletableFuture<Order> futureOrder = orderService.getOrderAsync(456); 
futureOrder.join();  // Wait for completion 
assertBean(futureOrder, "id,total", "456,99.99"); 
```

```java
// Test Result wrapper with custom swapper 
Result<User> result = userService.createUser(userData); 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertBean(result, "name,email", "Alice,alice@example.com"); 
 
// Test validation results 
ValidationResult<Order> validation = orderValidator.validate(order); 
    assertBean(validation, "id,total", "123,99.99"); 
 
// Test error case 
ValidationResult<Order> invalidValidation = orderValidator.validate(invalidOrder); 
    assertList(invalidValidation, "Missing required field: customer", "Invalid total: -10"); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
```

```java
// Test lazy computation 
LazyValue<Report> lazyReport = new LazyValue<>(() -> generateReport()); 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertBean(lazyReport, "title,itemCount", "Monthly Report,150"); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
 
// Swapper ensures the lazy value is evaluated before testing 
```

**Use Swappers when:**

- You need to unwrap or transform objects before property extraction
- The object is a wrapper type (Optional, Supplier, Result, etc.)
- You want to change the object structure for testing
- Property access needs to work on the unwrapped value

**Use Stringifiers when:**

- You only need to change the string representation
- The object structure is fine, just needs better formatting
- You want to customize how the object appears in assertions

Swappers are applied before any other processing:

```java
// 1. Swapper unwraps the Optional 
Optional<User> optUser = Optional.of(new User("Alice", 25)); 
 
// 2. PropertyExtractor accesses properties on the unwrapped User 
// 3. Stringifier formats the property values 
 
assertBean(optUser, "name,age", "Alice,25"); 
```

```java
// Ensure thread-safe swappers for concurrent testing 
Swapper<ThreadSafeLazy> threadSafeSwapper = (conv, lazy) -> { 
    if (lazy == null) return null; 
    synchronized (lazy) { 
        return lazy.getValue(); 
    } 
}; 
```

```java
// Robust swapper with error handling 
Swapper<AsyncValue> safeAsyncSwapper = (conv, async) -> { 
    if (async == null) return null; 
    try { 
        return async.get(1, TimeUnit.SECONDS); 
    } catch (TimeoutException e) { 
        return "&lt;timeout&gt;"; 
    } catch (Exception e) { 
        return "&lt;error: " + e.getMessage() + "&gt;"; 
    } 
}; 
```

- Domain-specific wrapper types
- Lazy evaluation containers
- Result/Either/Try types
- Async/Future-like types
- Proxy objects
- Validation wrappers

- Always handle null input appropriately
- Return null for null input (maintain null semantics)
- Keep swappers simple and focused on unwrapping/transformation
- Avoid expensive operations in swappers when possible
- Document any side effects (e.g., evaluating lazy values)
- Consider thread safety for shared swappers
- Handle exceptions gracefully with meaningful error messages

- Swappers are called for every object being tested
- Avoid expensive operations (database calls, network requests)
- Consider caching for expensive transformations
- Be careful with lazy evaluation - ensure it's what you want
- Use timeouts for async operations

```java
// Use descriptive names that indicate the transformation 
optionalSwapper()      // Unwraps Optional 
supplierSwapper()      // Evaluates Supplier 
futureSwapper()        // Extracts Future result 
resultSwapper()        // Unwraps Result type 
eitherSwapper()        // Extracts Either value 
validationSwapper()    // Unwraps ValidationResult 
```

```java
// Optional/Maybe 
Swapper<Optional> optionalSwapper = (conv, opt) ->  
    opt != null ? opt.orElse(null) : null; 
 
// Try/Result 
Swapper<Try> trySwapper = (conv, tryValue) ->  
    tryValue != null ? (tryValue.isSuccess() ? tryValue.get() : tryValue.getError()) : null; 
 
// Either 
Swapper<Either> eitherSwapper = (conv, either) ->  
    either != null ? (either.isLeft() ? either.getLeft() : either.getRight()) : null; 
```

```java
// CompletableFuture 
Swapper<CompletableFuture> futureSwapper = (conv, future) -> { 
    if (future == null) return null; 
    if (future.isDone()) { 
        try { 
            return future.get(); 
        } catch (Exception e) { 
            return "&lt;error&gt;"; 
        } 
    } 
    return "&lt;pending&gt;"; 
}; 
```

```java
// Validation result with errors 
Swapper<Validation> validationSwapper = (conv, validation) -> { 
    if (validation == null) return null; 
    return validation.isValid() ? validation.getValue() : validation.getErrors(); 
}; 
```

- [juneau-bct Basics](#topics-juneaubctbasics) - Main BCT documentation
- [Customization](#topics-customization) - Configuration via @BctConfig and BctConfiguration
  - [Stringifiers](#topics-stringifiers) - Converting objects to strings
  - [Listifiers](#topics-listifiers) - Converting collection-like objects to lists
- [PropertyExtractors](#topics-propertyextractors) - Custom property access logic

---

<a id="topics-propertyextractors"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/PropertyExtractors/ -->

<!-- page_index: 148 -->

# Property Extractors

Property extractors define custom property access logic for objects that don't follow standard JavaBean patterns or require specialized property extraction. They provide a flexible way to access properties from any object structure.

Property extractors are used when:

- Objects don't follow JavaBean getter conventions
- You need to compute properties dynamically
- You want to provide custom property names or aliases
- Objects use non-standard property access methods
- You need to access properties from legacy systems

Define custom property access logic by implementing the `PropertyExtractor` interface:

```java
// Database entity extractor 
PropertyExtractor entityExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return obj instanceof DatabaseEntity; 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        DatabaseEntity entity = (DatabaseEntity) obj; 
        switch (prop) { 
            case "id": return entity.getPrimaryKey(); 
            case "displayName": return entity.computeDisplayName(); 
            default: return entity.getAttribute(prop); 
        } 
    } 
}; 
 
// Registration 
var converter = BasicBeanConverter.builder() 
    .defaultSettings() 
    .addPropertyExtractor(entityExtractor) 
    .build(); 
```

```java
// Map-based property extractor with type conversion 
PropertyExtractor mapExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return obj instanceof Map; 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        Map<?, ?> map = (Map<?, ?>) obj; 
         
        // Support special properties 
        if ("size".equals(prop)) { 
            return map.size(); 
        } 
        if ("isEmpty".equals(prop)) { 
            return map.isEmpty(); 
        } 
        if ("keys".equals(prop)) { 
            return new ArrayList<>(map.keySet()); 
        } 
        if ("values".equals(prop)) { 
            return new ArrayList<>(map.values()); 
        } 
         
        // Standard key access 
        return map.get(prop); 
    } 
}; 
```

```java
// Extractor that provides property aliases 
PropertyExtractor aliasExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    private final Map<String, String> aliases = Map.of( 
        "fname", "firstName", 
        "lname", "lastName", 
        "email", "emailAddress", 
        "phone", "phoneNumber" 
    ); 
     
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return aliases.containsKey(prop); 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        String actualProp = aliases.get(prop); 
        try { 
            String methodName = "get" + capitalize(actualProp); 
            Method method = obj.getClass().getMethod(methodName); 
            return method.invoke(obj); 
        } catch (Exception e) { 
            throw new RuntimeException("Failed to extract property: " + prop, e); 
        } 
    } 
     
    private String capitalize(String s) { 
        return s.substring(0, 1).toUpperCase() + s.substring(1); 
    } 
}; 
 
// Usage 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertBean(user, "fname,lname,email", "John,Doe,john@example.com"); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
```

```java
// Extractor for computed/derived properties 
PropertyExtractor computedExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return obj instanceof User && prop.startsWith("computed_"); 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        User user = (User) obj; 
        switch (prop) { 
            case "computed_fullName": 
                return user.getFirstName() + " " + user.getLastName(); 
            case "computed_age": 
                return Period.between(user.getBirthDate(), LocalDate.now()).getYears(); 
            case "computed_initials": 
                return user.getFirstName().charAt(0) + "." + user.getLastName().charAt(0) + "."; 
            default: 
                throw new IllegalArgumentException("Unknown computed property: " + prop); 
        } 
    } 
}; 
 
// Usage 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertBean(user, "computed_fullName,computed_age,computed_initials",  
          "John Doe,30,J.D."); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
```

```java
// Extract properties from XML/JSON documents 
PropertyExtractor documentExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return obj instanceof JsonNode || obj instanceof XmlNode; 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        if (obj instanceof JsonNode) { 
            JsonNode node = (JsonNode) obj; 
            return node.get(prop); 
        } else if (obj instanceof XmlNode) { 
            XmlNode node = (XmlNode) obj; 
            return node.getAttribute(prop); 
        } 
        return null; 
    } 
}; 
```

```java
// Access private fields via reflection 
PropertyExtractor privateFieldExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return prop.startsWith("_");  // Private fields prefixed with underscore 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        try { 
            String fieldName = prop.substring(1);  // Remove underscore prefix 
            Field field = obj.getClass().getDeclaredField(fieldName); 
            field.setAccessible(true); 
            return field.get(obj); 
        } catch (Exception e) { 
            throw new RuntimeException("Failed to access field: " + prop, e); 
        } 
    } 
}; 
 
// Usage 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertBean(myBean, "_privateField1,_privateField2", "value1,value2"); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
```

```java
// Extract columns from ResultSet 
PropertyExtractor resultSetExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return obj instanceof ResultSet; 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        ResultSet rs = (ResultSet) obj; 
        try { 
            // Support column index access 
            if (prop.matches("\\d+")) { 
                int index = Integer.parseInt(prop); 
                return rs.getObject(index); 
            } 
            // Support column name access 
            return rs.getObject(prop); 
        } catch (SQLException e) { 
            throw new RuntimeException("Failed to extract column: " + prop, e); 
        } 
    } 
}; 
```

```java
// Extract properties from configuration objects 
PropertyExtractor configExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return obj instanceof Configuration; 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        Configuration config = (Configuration) obj; 
         
        // Support dot-notation for nested properties 
        if (prop.contains(".")) { 
            String[] parts = prop.split("\\."); 
            Object current = config; 
            for (String part : parts) { 
                current = ((Configuration) current).getProperty(part); 
                if (current == null) return null; 
            } 
            return current; 
        } 
         
        return config.getProperty(prop); 
    } 
}; 
 
// Usage 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertBean(config, "database.host,database.port,app.name",  
          "localhost,5432,MyApp"); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
```

- Non-standard property access patterns
- Legacy systems without JavaBean getters
- Computed or derived properties
- Property aliases or shortcuts
- Document/tree structures (XML, JSON)
- Database results or cursors
- Dynamic property systems

- Always implement `canExtract()` to check object compatibility
- Handle null objects and properties gracefully
- Throw descriptive exceptions for invalid properties
- Document property naming conventions
- Consider performance for frequently accessed properties
- Keep extraction logic simple and focused
- Avoid side effects or state modifications

```java
// Cache expensive computations 
PropertyExtractor cachedExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    private final Map<String, Object> cache = new ConcurrentHashMap<>(); 
     
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return obj instanceof ExpensiveObject; 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        String cacheKey = System.identityHashCode(obj) + ":" + prop; 
        return cache.computeIfAbsent(cacheKey, k -> { 
            // Expensive computation here 
            return ((ExpensiveObject) obj).computeProperty(prop); 
        }); 
    } 
}; 
```

```java
// Robust error handling in property extractors 
PropertyExtractor safeExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return obj instanceof MyObject; 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        try { 
            MyObject myObj = (MyObject) obj; 
            return myObj.getProperty(prop); 
        } catch (PropertyNotFoundException e) { 
            return "<not found>"; 
        } catch (Exception e) { 
            return "<error: " + e.getClass().getSimpleName() + ">"; 
        } 
    } 
}; 
```

```java
// Ensure thread-safe property extraction 
PropertyExtractor threadSafeExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return obj instanceof ThreadSafeObject; 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        ThreadSafeObject tso = (ThreadSafeObject) obj; 
        synchronized (tso) { 
            return tso.getProperty(prop); 
        } 
    } 
}; 
```

```java
// Multiple extractors chained together 
PropertyExtractor chainedExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    private final List<PropertyExtractor> extractors = Arrays.asList( 
        entityExtractor, 
        mapExtractor, 
        computedExtractor 
    ); 
     
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return extractors.stream() 
            .anyMatch(e -> e.canExtract(conv, obj, prop)); 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        for (PropertyExtractor extractor : extractors) { 
            if (extractor.canExtract(conv, obj, prop)) { 
                return extractor.extract(conv, obj, prop); 
            } 
        } 
        throw new IllegalArgumentException("No extractor found for property: " + prop); 
    } 
}; 
```

```java
// Try multiple extraction strategies with fallback 
PropertyExtractor fallbackExtractor = new PropertyExtractor() { 
    @Override 
    public boolean canExtract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        return true;  // Universal extractor 
    } 
     
    @Override 
    public Object extract(BeanConverter conv, Object obj, String prop) { 
        // Try getter method 
        try { 
            String methodName = "get" + capitalize(prop); 
            Method method = obj.getClass().getMethod(methodName); 
            return method.invoke(obj); 
        } catch (Exception e1) { 
            // Try field access 
            try { 
                Field field = obj.getClass().getDeclaredField(prop); 
                field.setAccessible(true); 
                return field.get(obj); 
            } catch (Exception e2) { 
                // Try map access 
                if (obj instanceof Map) { 
                    return ((Map<?, ?>) obj).get(prop); 
                } 
                return null; 
            } 
        } 
    } 
}; 
```

```java
// Test database entity 
DatabaseEntity entity = loadEntity(123); 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertBean(entity, "id,displayName,createdDate", "123,John Doe,2023-01-15"); 
 
// Test configuration 
Configuration config = loadConfig(); 
    assertBean(config, "database.host,database.port,app.timeout",  
          "localhost,5432,30000"); 
 
// Test computed properties 
User user = loadUser(456); 
    assertBean(user, "computed_fullName,computed_age", "Alice Smith,28"); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
```

```java
// Use property extractor with nested access 
BctAssertions.setConverter(converter); 
try { 
    assertBean(order, "customer{computed_fullName},items{0{name}}",  
          "{John Doe},{{Laptop}}"); 
} finally { 
    BctAssertions.resetConverter(); 
} 
```

- [juneau-bct Basics](#topics-juneaubctbasics) - Main BCT documentation
- [Customization](#topics-customization) - Configuration via @BctConfig and BctConfiguration
  - [Stringifiers](#topics-stringifiers) - Converting objects to strings
  - [Listifiers](#topics-listifiers) - Converting collection-like objects to lists
  - [Swappers](#topics-swappers) - Transforming objects before processing

<a id="topics-juneaurestcommonbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauRestCommonBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 149 -->

# juneau-rest-common Basics

```xml
<dependency> 
    <groupId>org.apache.juneau</groupId> 
    <artifactId>juneau-rest-common</artifactId> 
    <version>${juneau.version}</version> 
</dependency> 
```

```text
juneau-rest-common-0.0.0.jar 
```

```text
org.apache.juneau.rest.common_0.0.0.jar 
```

The [org.apache.juneau.http](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/package-summary.html) package contains a slew of useful extensions to the Apache
HttpComponents libraries and define APIs used extensively in the REST server and client APIs.

HTTP Packages[org.apache.juneau.http](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/package-summary.html) - Utility classes used for HTTP parts/headers/entities.[org.apache.juneau.http.annotation](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/package-summary.html) - Swagger-based annotations used on both server-side APIs and client-side proxy interfaces.[org.apache.juneau.http.entity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/entity/package-summary.html) - HTTP entity classes.[org.apache.juneau.http.header](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/package-summary.html) - HTTP header classes.[org.apache.juneau.http.part](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/package-summary.html) - HTTP query/form-data/path classes.[org.apache.juneau.http.remote](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/remote/package-summary.html) - Remote proxy interface API[org.apache.juneau.http.resource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/package-summary.html) - HTTP resource classes.[org.apache.juneau.http.response](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/package-summary.html) - HTTP response classes.

These APIs extend from the Apache HttpComponents libraries and can be used with libraries based on it such as Apache
HttpClient.
The REST Client API described later is built on top of Apache HttpClient and many of the classes defined in this package
make up integral components of that API.
Likewise, the APIs defined here are also used in the REST Server APIs also described later.

---

<a id="topics-helperclasses"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HelperClasses/ -->

<!-- page_index: 150 -->

# Helper Classes

The [org.apache.juneau.http.header](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/package-summary.html) package contains various convenience classes for creating
standard HTTP components using static imports.

[HttpHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html) - Utility class for standard HTTP headers.[HttpParts](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html) - Utility class for standard HTTP parts.[HttpEntities](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpEntities.html) - Utility class for standard HTTP entities.[HttpResources](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResources.html) - Utility class for standard HTTP resources.[HttpResponses](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html) - Utility class for standard HTTP resources.

The [HttpHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html) class contains many convenience static methods
and fields for working with standard HTTP request and response headers and header lists.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpHeaders.*;
>
> HeaderList headers =
>     headerList(  // Arbitrary list of headers
>         CONTENTTYPE_TEXT_XML,   // Static constants
>         contentType("text/xml")   // Predefined headers
>         contentType(() -> "text/xml")   // Predefined headers with supplied values
>         stringHeader("Content-Type", "text/xml")   // Freeform headers
>         stringHeader("Content-Type", () -> "text/xml")   // Freeform headers with supplied values
>     );
> ```

This class is vast in scope and covers all request and response headers defined in RFC2616.
In addition to the predefined headers, various methods are provided for free-form headers.
Each accepts either static values or values from [Suppliers](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/function/Supplier.html):

[HttpHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html)
[basicHeader(String,Object)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#basicHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.Object))[booleanHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#booleanHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.Boolean))[csvHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#csvHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.String))[dateHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#dateHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.String))[entityTagsHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#entityTagsHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.String))[entityTagHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#entityTagHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.String))[integerHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#integerHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.Integer))[longHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#longHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.Long))[mediaRangesHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#mediaRangesHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.String))[mediaTypeHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#mediaTypeHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.String))[stringHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#stringHeader(java.lang.String))[serializedHeader(String,Object)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#serializedHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.Object))[stringRangesHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#stringRangesHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.String))[uriHeader(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#uriHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.String))

The [serializedHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#serializedHeader(java.lang.String,java.lang.Object)) methods
allows for headers serialized using schema-based serializers such as the OpenAPI serializer.

Static methods are also provided for instantiating [Header](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Header.html)-annotated or other `HttpComponent`-defined header classes:

[HttpHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html)
[header(Class,String,Object)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#eTag(org.apache.juneau.http.header.EntityTag))[header(Class,Object)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#eTag(org.apache.juneau.http.header.EntityTag))

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpHeaders.*;
>
> ContentType contentType = header(ContentType.class, "text/xml");
> ```

Lists of headers can be produced with the following methods:

[HttpHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html)
[headerList()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#headerList())[headerList(Header...)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#headerList())[headerList(List)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#headerList())[headerList(String...)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#headerList())

The capabilities of the [HeaderList](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html) class is described
later.

The [HttpParts](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html) class contains convenience static methods for
generating query/form-data/path parts and part lists.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpParts.*;
>
> PartList formData =
>     partList(  // Arbitrary list of parts
>         stringPart("Name", "Bill")   // Freeform part
>         integerPart("Age", () -> calculateAge())   // Freeform part with supplied value
>     );
> ```

The following methods are provided for creating parts.
Each accepts either static values or values from [Suppliers](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/function/Supplier.html):

[HttpParts](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html)
[basicPart(String,Object)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#basicPart(java.lang.String))[booleanPart(String,Boolean)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#booleanPart(java.lang.String,java.lang.Boolean))[csvArrayPart(String,String...)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#csvArrayPart(java.lang.String,java.lang.String...))[datePart(String,ZonedDateTime)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#datePart(java.lang.String,java.time.ZonedDateTime))[integerPart(String,Integer)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#integerPart(java.lang.String,java.lang.Integer))[longPart(String,Long)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#longPart(java.lang.String,java.lang.Long))[serializedPart(String,Object)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#serializedPart(java.lang.String,java.lang.Object))[stringPart(String,String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#stringPart(java.lang.String,java.lang.String))[uriPart(String,URI)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#uriPart(java.lang.String,java.net.URI))

The [serializedPart](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#serializedPart(java.lang.String,java.lang.Object)) methods allows
for parts serialized using schema-based serializers such as the OpenAPI serializer.

Lists of parts can be produced with the following methods:

[HttpParts](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html)
[partList()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#partList())[partList(List)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#partList())[partList(NameValuePair...)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#partList())[partList(String...)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html#partList())

The capabilities of the [PartList](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html) class is described later.

The [HttpEntities](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpEntities.html) class contains convenience static methods for
generating HTTP message entities.

Returned objects extend from `org.apache.http.HttpEntity` but provides the following additional features:

- Caching.
- Fluent setters.
- Fluent assertions.
- Externally-supplied/dynamic content.

The following methods are provided for creating entities.
Each accepts either static values or values from [Suppliers](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/function/Supplier.html) and returns
builders:

[HttpEntities](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpEntities.html)
[byteArrayEntity(byte[])](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpEntities.html#byteArrayEntity(byte[]))[fileEntity(File)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpEntities.html#fileEntity(java.io.File))[readerEntity(Reader)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpEntities.html#readerEntity(java.io.Reader))[serializedEntity(Object, Serializer)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpEntities.html#serializedEntity(java.lang.Object,org.apache.juneau.serializer.Serializer))[streamEntity(InputStream)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpEntities.html#streamEntity(java.io.InputStream))[stringEntity(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpEntities.html#stringEntity(java.lang.String))

HTTP entities are automatically supported in both the server and client REST APIs for requests and responses.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpResources.*;
>
> @RestDelete(path="/{id}")
> public HttpEntity helloWold(...) {
>     return stringEntity("Hello!").contentType("text/plain");
> }
> ```

The [HttpResources](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResources.html) class contains convenience static methods
for generating HTTP message resources.
Returned objects extend from [HttpResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/HttpResource.html) which
extends from [HttpEntity](https://hc.apache.org/httpcomponents-core-4.4.x/current/httpcore/apidocs/org/apache/http/HttpEntity.html) but with additional arbitrary headers.

The following methods are provided for creating entities.
Each accepts either static values or values from [Suppliers](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/function/Supplier.html) and are in
the form of builders.

[HttpResources](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResources.html)
[byteArrayResource(byte[])](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResources.html#byteArrayResource(byte[]))[fileResource(File)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResources.html#fileResource(java.io.File))[readerResource(Reader)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResources.html#readerResource(java.io.Reader))[streamResource(InputStream)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResources.html#streamResource(java.io.InputStream))[stringResource(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResources.html#stringResource(java.lang.String))

The most common location where resources are used are as returned types of REST operation methods described later.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpResources.*;
>
> @RestDelete(path="/{id}")
> public HttpResource helloWold(...) {
>     return stringResource("Hello!").contentType("text/plain").header("Cache-Control", "none");
> }
> ```

The [HttpResponses](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html) class contains convenience static methods
for standard HTTP responses.
Returned objects extend from `org.apache.http.HttpResponse` and are in the form of builders.
The following methods are provided for creating entities:

[HttpResponses](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html) [\_continue](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#_continue()) [accepted](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#accepted()) [alreadyReported](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#alreadyReported()) [badRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#badRequest()) [conflict](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#conflict()) [created](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#created()) [earlyHints](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#earlyHints()) [expectationFailed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#expectationFailed()) [failedDependency](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#failedDependency()) [forbidden](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#forbidden()) [found](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#found(java.lang.String)) [gone](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#gone()) [httpVersionNotSupported](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#httpVersionNotSupported()) [imUsed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#imUsed()) [insufficientStorage](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#insufficientStorage()) [internalServerError](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#internalServerError()) [lengthRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#lengthRequired()) [locked](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#locked()) [loopDetected](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#loopDetected()) [methodNotAllowed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#methodNotAllowed()) [misdirectedRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#misdirectedRequest()) [movedPermanently](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#movedPermanently(java.lang.String)) [multipleChoices](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#multipleChoices()) [multiStatus](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#multiStatus()) [networkAuthenticationRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#networkAuthenticationRequired()) [noContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#noContent()) [nonAuthoritiveInformation](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#nonAuthoritiveInformation()) [notAcceptable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#notAcceptable()) [notExtended](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#notExtended()) [notFound](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#notFound()) [notImplemented](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#notImplemented()) [notModified](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#notModified()) [ok](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#ok()) [partialContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#partialContent()) [payloadTooLarge](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#payloadTooLarge()) [permanentRedirect](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#permanentRedirect(java.lang.String)) [preconditionFailed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#preconditionFailed()) [preconditionRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#preconditionRequired()) [processing](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#processing()) [rangeNotSatisfiable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#rangeNotSatisfiable()) [requestHeaderFieldsTooLarge](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#requestHeaderFieldsTooLarge()) [resetContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#resetContent()) [seeOther](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#seeOther(java.lang.String)) [serviceUnavailable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#serviceUnavailable()) [switchingProtocols](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#switchingProtocols()) [temporaryRedirect](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#temporaryRedirect(java.lang.String)) [tooManyRequests](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#tooManyRequests()) [unauthorized](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#unauthorized()) [unavailableForLegalReasons](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#unavailableForLegalReasons()) [unprocessableEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#unprocessableEntity()) [unsupportedMediaType](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#unsupportedMediaType()) [upgradeRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#upgradeRequired()) [uriTooLong](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#uriTooLong()) [useProxy](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#useProxy()) [variantAlsoNegotiates](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpResponses.html#variantAlsoNegotiates())

The most common location where these responses are used are in REST operation methods described later.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpResponses.*;
> import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpHeaders.*;
>
> @RestDelete(path="/{id}")
> public Ok doDelete(...) throws Unauthorized {
>     if (/* user not authorized*/)
>         throw unauthorized();
>     return ok().content("Delete was successful");
> }
> ```

---

<a id="topics-annotations"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Annotations/ -->

<!-- page_index: 151 -->

# Annotations

The [org.apache.juneau.http.annotation](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/package-summary.html) package contains annotations for defining both
server and client side APIs.

The server-side APIs also use it for producing auto-generated Swagger documentation through the REST API itself.

[Contact](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Contact.html) [Content](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Content.html) [FormData](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/FormData.html) [HasFormData](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/HasFormData.html) [HasQuery](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/HasQuery.html) [Header](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Header.html) [License](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/License.html) [Path](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Path.html) [Query](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Query.html) [Request](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Request.html) [Response](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Response.html) [StatusCode](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/StatusCode.html) [Tag](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Tag.html)

These annotations are used in a variety of places in the server and client side REST interfaces, especially for remote
proxies.
These will be described later in those sections.

---

<a id="topics-httpheaders"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HttpHeaders/ -->

<!-- page_index: 152 -->

# HTTP Headers

The [org.apache.juneau.http.header](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/package-summary.html) package contains implementations of
`org.apache.http.Header` for all common HTTP headers.

[Accept](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Accept.html) [AcceptCharset](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/AcceptCharset.html) [AcceptEncoding](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/AcceptEncoding.html) [AcceptLanguage](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/AcceptLanguage.html) [AcceptRanges](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/AcceptRanges.html) [Age](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Age.html) [Allow](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Allow.html) [Authorization](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Authorization.html) [CacheControl](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/CacheControl.html) [ClientVersion](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ClientVersion.html) [Connection](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Connection.html) [ContentDisposition](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ContentDisposition.html) [ContentEncoding](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ContentEncoding.html) [ContentLanguage](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ContentLanguage.html) [ContentLength](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ContentLength.html) [ContentLocation](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ContentLocation.html) [ContentRange](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ContentRange.html) [ContentType](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ContentType.html) [Date](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Date.html) [Debug](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Debug.html) [ETag](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ETag.html) [Expect](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Expect.html) [Expires](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Expires.html) [Forwarded](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Forwarded.html) [From](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/From.html) [Host](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Host.html) [IfMatch](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/IfMatch.html) [IfModifiedSince](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/IfModifiedSince.html) [IfNoneMatch](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/IfNoneMatch.html) [IfRange](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/IfRange.html) [IfUnmodifiedSince](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/IfUnmodifiedSince.html) [LastModified](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/LastModified.html) [Location](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Location.html) [MaxForwards](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/MaxForwards.html) [NoTrace](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/NoTrace.html) [Origin](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Origin.html) [Pragma](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Pragma.html) [ProxyAuthenticate](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ProxyAuthenticate.html) [ProxyAuthorization](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/ProxyAuthorization.html) [Range](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Range.html) [Referer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Referer.html) [RetryAfter](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/RetryAfter.html) [Server](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Server.html) [TE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/TE.html) [Thrown](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Thrown.html) [Trailer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Trailer.html) [TransferEncoding](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/TransferEncoding.html) [Upgrade](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Upgrade.html) [UserAgent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/UserAgent.html) [Vary](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Vary.html) [Via](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Via.html) [Warning](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Warning.html) [WwwAuthenticate](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/WwwAuthenticate.html)

These headers extend from the following classes that provide data-type specific functionality:

[NameValuePair](https://hc.apache.org/httpcomponents-core-4.4.x/current/httpcore/apidocs/org/apache/http/NameValuePair.html)
[Header](https://hc.apache.org/httpcomponents-core-4.4.x/current/httpcore/apidocs/org/apache/http/Header.html)
[BasicHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicHeader.html)
[BasicBooleanHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicBooleanHeader.html) [BasicCsvHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicCsvHeader.html) [BasicDateHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicDateHeader.html) [BasicEntityTagHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicEntityTagHeader.html) [BasicEntityTagsHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicEntityTagsHeader.html) [BasicIntegerHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicIntegerHeader.html) [BasicLongHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicLongHeader.html) [BasicMediaRangesHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicMediaRangesHeader.html) [BasicMediaTypeHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicMediaTypeHeader.html) [BasicStringHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicStringHeader.html) [BasicStringRangesHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicStringRangesHeader.html) [BasicUriHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/BasicUriHeader.html)

Static methods are provided on [HttpHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html) to simplify creation
of headers:

[HttpHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html)
[accept(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#accept(java.lang.String))[authorization(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#authorization(java.lang.String))[contentType(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#contentType(java.lang.String))[userAgent(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html#userAgent(java.lang.String))

These subclasses provide various convenience methods to allow for easy fluent-style coding.

> [!TIP]
> **Examples**
> ```java
> // Validates the response body content is not expired.
> restClient
>     .get(URL)
>     .run()
>     .getHeader("Expires").asDateHeader().assertZonedDateTime().isLessThan(new Date());
> ```

The [HeaderList](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html) class is a list of HTTP headers.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Construct using builder.
> HeaderList headers = HeaderList
>     .create()
>     .append(Accept.of("text/xml"))
>     .append("Content-Type", ()->getDynamicContentTypeFromSomewhere());
>
> // Construct using convenience creator.
> HeaderList headers = HeaderList.of(Accept.TEXT_XML, ContentType.TEXT_XML);
> ```

Static methods are provided on [HttpHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpHeaders.html) to further simplify
creation of header lists.

```java
import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpHeaders.*; 
 
HeaderList headers = headerList(accept("text/xml"), contentType("text/xml")); 
```

The builder class supports setting default header values (i.e. add a header to the list if it isn't otherwise in the
list).
Note that this is different from simply setting a value twice as using default values will not overwrite existing
headers.

The following example notes the distinction:

```java
headers = HeaderList 
    .create() 
    .set(Accept.TEXT_PLAIN) 
    .set(Accept.TEXT_XML); 
assertObject(headers).isString("[Accept: text/xml]"); 
 
headers = HeaderList 
    .create() 
    .set(Accept.TEXT_PLAIN) 
    .setDefault(Accept.TEXT_XML); 
assertObject(headers).isString("[Accept: text/plain]"); 
```

Various methods are provided for iterating over the headers in this list to avoid array copies.

[HeaderList](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html)
[forEach(Consumer)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#forEach(java.lang.String,java.util.function.Consumer))[forEach(String,Consumer)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#forEach(java.lang.String,java.util.function.Consumer))[forEach(Predicate,Consumer)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#forEach(java.lang.String,java.util.function.Consumer))[headerIterator()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#headerIterator())[headerIterator(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#headerIterator())[stream()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#stream(java.lang.String))[stream(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#stream(java.lang.String))[getAll()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#getAll())[getAll(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#getAll())[get(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#get(java.lang.Class))[get(Class)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#get(java.lang.Class))[get(String,Class)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#get(java.lang.Class))

In general, try to use these over the [getAll()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#getAll()) / [getAll(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#getAll()) methods that require array copies.

Similar to the way multiple parts can be collapsed into a single value, the [get(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#get(java.lang.Class)) method is special in that it will collapse multiple headers with the same name into a single comma-delimited list (see RFC 2616 Section 4.2 for rules).

The [get(Class)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#get(java.lang.Class)) and [get(String,Class)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#get(java.lang.Class)) methods are provided for working with [Header](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Header.html)-annotated beans.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> HeaderList headers = HeaderList.of(Accept.TEXT_JSON, Accept.TEXT_XML);
>
> // Returns "text/json, text/xml"
> Accept accept = headers.get(Accept.class);
> ```

By default, header names are treated as case-insensitive.
This can be changed using the [caseSensitive(boolean)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html#caseSensitive(boolean)) method.

A [VarResolver](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.html) can be associated with this builder to create
header values with embedded variables that are resolved at runtime.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Create a header list with dynamically-resolving values pulled from a system property.
>
> System.setProperty("foo", "bar");
>
> HeaderList headers = HeaderList
>     .create()
>     .resolving()
>     .append("X1", "$S{foo}")
>     .append("X2", ()->"$S{foo}");
>
> assertObject(headers).isString("[X1: bar, X2: bar]");
> ```

The [HeaderList](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/HeaderList.html) object can be extended to defined
pre-packaged lists of headers which can be used in various annotations throughout the framework.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // A predefined list of headers.
> public class MyHeaderList extends HeaderList {
>     public MyHeaderList() {
>         super(Accept.TEXT_XML, ContentType.TEXT_XML);
>     }
> }
>
> // Use it on a remote proxy to add headers on all requests.
> @Remote(path="/petstore", headerList=MyHeaderList.class)
> public interface PetStore {
>
>     @RemotePost("/pets")
>     Pet addPet(
>         @Content CreatePet createPet,
>         @Header("E-Tag") UUID etag,
>         @Query("debug") boolean debug
>     );
> }
> ```

---

<a id="topics-httpparts"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HttpParts/ -->

<!-- page_index: 153 -->

# HTTP Parts

The [org.apache.juneau.http.part](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/package-summary.html) package contains implementations of
`org.apache.http.NameValuePair` to be used for query/form-data/path parts and part lists.

[NameValuePair](https://hc.apache.org/httpcomponents-core-4.4.x/current/httpcore/apidocs/org/apache/http/NameValuePair.html)
[BasicPart](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/BasicPart.html)
[BasicBooleanPart](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/BasicBooleanPart.html)
[BasicCsvArrayPart](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/BasicCsvArrayPart.html)
[BasicDatePart](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/BasicDatePart.html)
[BasicIntegerPart](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/BasicIntegerPart.html)
[BasicLongPart](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/BasicLongPart.html)
[BasicPartIterator](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/BasicPartIterator.html)
[BasicStringPart](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/BasicStringPart.html)
[BasicUriPart](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/BasicUriPart.html)

The [PartList](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html) class is a list of HTTP parts (form-data, query-parameters, path-parameters).

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> PartList parts = PartList
>     .create()
>     .append(MyPart.of("foo"))
>     .append("Bar", ()->getDynamicValueFromSomewhere());
> ```

Convenience creators are provided for creating lists with minimal code:

```java
PartList parts = PartList.of(BasicIntegerPart.of("foo", 1)); 
```

Static methods are provided on [HttpParts](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/HttpParts.html) to further simplify
creation of part lists.

```java
import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpParts.*; 
 
PartList parts = partList(integerPart("foo", 1), booleanPart("bar", false)); 
```

The builder class supports setting default part values (i.e. add a part to the list if it isn't otherwise in the list).
Note that this is different from simply setting a value twice as using default values will not overwrite existing parts.

The following example notes the distinction:

```java
parts = PartList 
    .create() 
    .set("Foo", "bar") 
    .set("Foo", "baz"); 
assertObject(parts).isString("foo=baz"); 
 
parts = PartList 
    .create() 
    .set("Foo", "bar") 
    .setDefault("Foo", "baz"); 
assertObject(parts).isString("foo=bar"); 
```

Various methods are provided for iterating over the parts in this list to avoid array copies.

- [forEach(Consumer)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#forEach(java.lang.String,java.util.function.Consumer)) / [forEach(String,Consumer)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#forEach(java.lang.String,java.util.function.Consumer)) / [forEach(Predicate,Consumer)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#forEach(java.lang.String,java.util.function.Consumer)) - Use consumers to process parts.
- [partIterator()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#partIterator()) / [partIterator(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#partIterator()) - Use an [PartIterator](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartIterator.html) to process parts.
- [stream()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#stream(java.lang.String)) / [stream(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#stream(java.lang.String)) - Use a stream.

In general, try to use these over the [getAll()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#getAll()) / [getAll(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#getAll()) methods that require array copies.
Similar to the way multiple headers can be collapsed into a single value, the [get(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#get(java.lang.Class)) method is special in that it will collapse multiple parts with the same name into a single comma-delimited list.

The [get(Class)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#get(java.lang.Class)) and [get(String,Class)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html#get(java.lang.Class)) methods are provided for working with [FormData](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/FormData.html) / [Query](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Query.html) / [Path](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Path.html)-annotated beans.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> MyQueryBean foo = parts.get(MyQueryBean.class);
> ```

A [VarResolver](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolver.html) can be associated with this builder to create part
values with embedded variables that are resolved at runtime.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Create a part list with dynamically-resolving values pulled from a system property.
>
> System.setProperty("foo", "bar");
>
> PartList parts = PartList
>     .create()
>     .resolving()
>     .append("X1", "$S{foo}")
>     .append("X2", ()->"$S{foo}");
>
> assertObject(parts).isString("X1=bar&X2=bar");
> ```

The [PartList](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/part/PartList.html) object can be extended to defined pre-packaged
lists of parts which can be used in various annotations throughout the framework.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // A predefined list of parts.
> public class MyPartList extends PartList {
>     public MyPartList() {
>         super(BasicIntegerPart.of("foo",1), BasicBooleanPart.of("bar",false));
>     }
> }
> ```

---

<a id="topics-httpentitiesandresources"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HttpEntitiesAndResources/ -->

<!-- page_index: 154 -->

# HTTP Entities and Resources

The [org.apache.juneau.http.entity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/entity/package-summary.html) package contains implementations of
`org.apache.http.HttpEntity`.
These are entities that can be sent or received with an HTTP message.
They consist of a message body and the headers `Content-Type`, `Content-Length`, and `Content-Encoding`.

The [org.apache.juneau.http.resource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/package-summary.html) package contains implementations of [HttpResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/HttpResource.html) which are extensions of `org.apache.http.HttpEntity` with arbitrary headers added beyond the standard content headers.

[HttpEntity](https://hc.apache.org/httpcomponents-core-4.4.x/current/httpcore/apidocs/org/apache/http/HttpEntity.html)
[BasicHttpEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/entity/BasicHttpEntity.html)
[ByteArrayEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/entity/ByteArrayEntity.html) [FileEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/entity/FileEntity.html) [StreamEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/entity/StreamEntity.html) [ReaderEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/entity/ReaderEntity.html) [SerializedEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/entity/SerializedEntity.html) [StringEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/entity/StringEntity.html)
[HttpResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/HttpResource.html)
[BasicResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/BasicResource.html)
[ByteArrayResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/ByteArrayResource.html) [FileResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/FileResource.html) [StreamResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/StreamResource.html) [ReaderResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/ReaderResource.html) [StringResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/StringResource.html)

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> import static org.apache.juneau.http.HttpEntities.*;
>
