> byte[] payload = {...};
>
> // Create via type builder.
> HttpEntity entity = ByteArrayEntity
>     .create()
>     .content(payload)
>     .contentType(ContentType.APPLICATION_OCTET_STREAM);
>
> // Create via HttpEntities.
> HttpEntity entity = byteArrayEntity(payload, ContentType.APPLICATION_OCTET_STREAM);
> ```

HTTP entities and resources can be used by both the server and client side APIs described in later sections.

```java
// REST endpoint that simply echos an HTTP entity. 
@RestPost(path="/entity") 
public HttpEntity echoMyEntity(HttpEntity entity) { 
    return entity; 
} 
 
// REST endpoint that serves up a static file. 
@RestGet(path="/resource/{fileName}") 
public HttpResource getStaticFile(@Path String fileName, Locale locale) { 
    getContext().getStaticFiles().resolve(fileName, locale).orElseThrow(NotFound::new); 
} 
```

```java
// REST client that uses the echo REST endpoint above. 
 
HttpEntity entity = byteArrayEntity(...); 
 
entity = RestClient.create() 
    .build() 
    .rootUrl(URI) 
    .post("/entity", entity) 
    .run() 
    .assertStatus().asCode().is(200) 
    .getContent().as(ByteArrayEntity.class); 
```

---

<a id="topics-httpresponses"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HttpResponses/ -->

<!-- page_index: 155 -->

# HTTP Responses

The [org.apache.juneau.http.response](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/package-summary.html) package contains predefined
`org.apache.http.HttpResponse` implementations for most standard HTTP responses.

[Accepted](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Accepted.html) [AlreadyReported](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/AlreadyReported.html) [BadRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/BadRequest.html) [Conflict](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Conflict.html) [Continue](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Continue.html) [Created](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Created.html) [EarlyHints](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/EarlyHints.html) [ExpectationFailed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/ExpectationFailed.html) [FailedDependency](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/FailedDependency.html) [Forbidden](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Forbidden.html) [Found](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Found.html) [Gone](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Gone.html) [HttpVersionNotSupported](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/HttpVersionNotSupported.html) [IMUsed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/IMUsed.html) [InsufficientStorage](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/InsufficientStorage.html) [InternalServerError](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/InternalServerError.html) [LengthRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/LengthRequired.html) [Locked](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Locked.html) [LoopDetected](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/LoopDetected.html) [MethodNotAllowed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MethodNotAllowed.html) [MisdirectedRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MisdirectedRequest.html) [MovedPermanently](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MovedPermanently.html) [MultipleChoices](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MultipleChoices.html) [MultiStatus](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MultiStatus.html) [NetworkAuthenticationRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NetworkAuthenticationRequired.html) [NoContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NoContent.html) [NonAuthoritiveInformation](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NonAuthoritiveInformation.html) [NotAcceptable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotAcceptable.html) [NotExtended](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotExtended.html) [NotFound](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotFound.html) [NotImplemented](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotImplemented.html) [NotModified](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotModified.html) [Ok](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Ok.html) [PartialContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PartialContent.html) [PayloadTooLarge](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PayloadTooLarge.html) [PermanentRedirect](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PermanentRedirect.html) [PreconditionFailed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PreconditionFailed.html) [PreconditionRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PreconditionRequired.html) [Processing](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Processing.html) [RangeNotSatisfiable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/RangeNotSatisfiable.html) [RequestHeaderFieldsTooLarge](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/RequestHeaderFieldsTooLarge.html) [ResetContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/ResetContent.html) [SeeOther](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/SeeOther.html) [ServiceUnavailable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/ServiceUnavailable.html) [SwitchingProtocols](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/SwitchingProtocols.html) [TemporaryRedirect](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/TemporaryRedirect.html) [TooManyRequests](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/TooManyRequests.html) [Unauthorized](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Unauthorized.html) [UnavailableForLegalReasons](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UnavailableForLegalReasons.html) [UnprocessableEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UnprocessableEntity.html) [UnsupportedMediaType](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UnsupportedMediaType.html) [UpgradeRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UpgradeRequired.html) [UriTooLong](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UriTooLong.html) [UseProxy](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UseProxy.html) [VariantAlsoNegotiates](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/VariantAlsoNegotiates.html)

These are built upon existing `HttpComponent` APIs:

`org.apache.http.HttpMessage``org.apache.http.HttpResponse`[BasicHttpResponse](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/BasicHttpResponse.html) - 100-399 response codes[BasicHttpException](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/BasicHttpException.html) - 400+ response codes

The most common location where these responses are used are in REST operation methods described later.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @RestDelete(path="/{id}")
> public Ok doDelete(@Path int id) throws NotFound, Unauthorized {
>     pojoService.delete(pojoService.find(id).orElseThrow(NotFound::new));
>     return Ok.OK;
> }
> ```

The following classes are also provided for constructing your own custom responses:

[BasicHttpException](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/BasicHttpException.html)
[BasicHttpResponse](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/BasicHttpResponse.html)

---

<a id="topics-remoteproxyinterfaces"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RemoteProxyInterfaces/ -->

<!-- page_index: 156 -->

# Remote Proxy Interfaces

The [org.apache.juneau.http.remote](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/remote/package-summary.html) package contains the annotations used for defining
client-side remote proxies.

[Remote](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/remote/Remote.html)
[RemoteGet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/remote/RemoteGet.html)
[RemotePut](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/remote/RemotePut.html)
[RemotePost](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/remote/RemotePost.html)
[RemoteDelete](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/remote/RemoteDelete.html)
[RemoteOp](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/remote/RemoteOp.html)

See [Proxies](#topics-remoteproxyinterfaces) for more information on use of these annotations.

---

<a id="topics-juneaurestserverbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauRestServerBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 157 -->

# juneau-rest-server Basics

```xml
<dependency> 
    <groupId>org.apache.juneau</groupId> 
    <artifactId>juneau-rest-server</artifactId> 
    <version>${juneau.version}</version> 
</dependency> 
```

```text
juneau-rest-server-0.0.0.jar 
```

```text
org.apache.juneau.rest.server_0.0.0.jar 
```

The `juneau-rest-server` library allows you to quickly wrap POJOs and expose them as full-fledged REST resources served up
in a servlet container using a bare-minimum amount of code.

The primary goal for Juneau was to make it as easy as possible to implement easy-to-read and self-documenting REST
resources using very little code.

One of the biggest advantages of the Juneau REST framework over similar architectures is that it hides the serialization
layer from the developer.

The developer can work entirely with POJOs and let the Juneau framework handle all the serialization and parsing work.

The developer need never know what the `Accept` or `Content-Type` or `Accept-Encoding` (etc...) header values are
because those details are all handled by the framework.

The API builds upon the existing JEE Servlet API.

The root class, [RestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html) is nothing but a specialized [HttpServlet](https://jakarta.ee/specifications/servlet/6.0/apidocs/jakarta/servlet/http/HttpServlet.html), and the [RestRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestRequest.html) and [RestResponse](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestResponse.html)
classes are nothing more than specialized [HttpServletRequest](https://jakarta.ee/specifications/servlet/6.0/apidocs/jakarta/servlet/http/HttpServletRequest.html)
and [HttpServletResponse](https://jakarta.ee/specifications/servlet/6.0/apidocs/jakarta/servlet/http/HttpServletResponse.html) objects.

This allows maximum flexibility for the developer since you can let Juneau handle operations such as serialization, or
you can revert to the existing servlet APIs to do low-level processing of requests yourself.

It also means you need nothing more than a Servlet container such as Jetty to use the REST framework.

- Deployable in standard Servlet containers.
- Deployable in Spring Boot environments with full support for injected beans.
- Serializes POJOs to JSON, XML, HTML, URL-Encoding, UON, RDF/XML, N-Triple, Turtle, N3, SOAP, or
  Java-serialized-object based on value of Accept header.
  No user code is required to handle these types.
  - Extensible design that provides ability to override existing content type handlers, or add the ability to
    handle other kinds of content types.
- Parses content of `POST/PUT` request bodies to POJOs.
- Automatic built-in ability to serialize POJO metadata to JSON+SCHEMA, XML+SCHEMA, or HTML+SCHEMA based on
  `Accept` header.
- Automatic negotiation of output Writer based on HTTP headers.
  - Automatic handling of `Accept-Charset` header for all character sets supported by the JVM.
  - Automatic handling of `Accept-Encoding` header with registered encoders.
- Automatic error handling.
  - Automatic 401 errors (Unauthorized) on failed guards.
  - Automatic 404 errors (Not Found) on unmatched path patterns.
  - Automatic 405 errors (Method Not Implemented) on unimplemented methods.
  - Automatic 406 errors (Not Acceptable) when no matching serializer was found to handle the Accept header.
  - Automatic 412 errors (Precondition Failed) when all matchers failed to match.
  - Automatic 415 errors (Unsupported Media Type) when no matching parser was found was found to handle the Content-Type header.
  - Automatic 500 errors on uncaught exceptions.
- Support for parsing all HTTP parts (`headers`, `query`, `formData`, `path` variables) using `Swagger` formatting rules
  and validations.
  Not limited to simple POJOs but rather you can represent arbitrarily-complex POJOs in any HTTP part using UON
  notation.
- Auto-created Swagger JSON and Swagger UI available through `OPTIONS` requests of resources.
- Various useful debugging features that make debugging using a browser extremely simple...
  - Ability to pass HTTP header values as URL `GET` parameters (e.g. `&Accept=text/xml`).
  - Ability to pass HTTP content on `PUT/POST` requests as a URL GET parameter (e.g. `&content=(foo=bar)`).
  - Ability to simulate non-`GET` requests using a `&method` GET parameter (e.g. `&method=POST`).
  - Ability to force `text/plain` on response using GET parameter `&plainText=true`.
- Ability to implement overloaded HTTP methods through the use of the `&method` attribute (e.g. `&method=FOO`).
- Ability to match URL patterns (e.g. `/foo/{fooId}/bar/{barId}`) against URLs
  (e.g. `/foo/123/bar/456/bing`).
- Ability to associate guards at the resource or method levels through annotations.
  Typically useful for security but can be used for a variety of purposes.
- Ability to associate converters at the resource or method levels through annotations.
  Typically useful for performing conversions on input and output, such as for supporting older input and output
  formats.

Many of the examples in this document are pulled directly from [juneau-examples-rest](#topics-juneaurestserverbasics).

---

<a id="topics-restserveroverview"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerOverview/ -->

<!-- page_index: 158 -->

# REST Server Overview

A REST resource is simply a Java class annotated with [@Rest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html).
The most common case is a class that extends [BasicRestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestServlet.html), which itself is simply an extension of [HttpServlet](https://jakarta.ee/specifications/servlet/6.0/apidocs/jakarta/servlet/http/HttpServlet.html) which allows it to be deployed as a servlet.

Juneau has two sample applications for demonstrating how to use the REST API, one using Jetty and one using Spring Boot:

`org.apache.juneau.examples.rest.jetty``org.apache.juneau.examples.rest.springboot`

The `org.apache.juneau.examples.rest.springboot` application is described in the section SpringBoot Overview.

The Jetty application consists of the following application class that registers our top-level servlet:

```java
public class App { 
 
    public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception { 
        JettyMicroservice 
            .create() 
            .args(args) 
            .servlet(RootResources.class) 
            .build() 
            .start() 
            .startConsole() 
            .join(); 
    } 
} 
```

The root resources class is an example of a router page that is used to attach children to:

```java
@Rest( 
    title="Root resources", 
    description="Example of a router resource page.", 
    children={ 
        HelloWorldResource.class, 
        DtoExamples.class, 
        UtilityBeansResource.class, 
        HtmlBeansResource.class, 
        ConfigResource.class, 
        ShutdownResource.class 
    } 
) 
@HtmlDocConfig( 
    widgets={ 
        ContentTypeMenuItem.class 
    }, 
    navlinks={ 
        "api: servlet:/api", 
        "stats: servlet:/stats", 
        "$W{ContentTypeMenuItem}", 
        "source: $C{Source/gitHub}/org/apache/juneau/examples/rest/RootResources.java" 
    }, 
    aside={ 
        "<div class='text'>", 
        "    <p>This is an example of a 'router' page that serves as a jumping-off point to child resources.</p>", 
        "    <p>Resources can be nested arbitrarily deep through router pages.</p>", 
        "    <p>Note the <span class='link'>API</span> link provided that lets you see the generated swagger doc for this page.</p>", 
        "    <p>Also note the <span class='link'>STATS</span> link to view runtime statistics on this page.</p>", 
        "    <p>Also note the <span class='link'>SOURCE</span> link to view the source code for the page.</p>", 
        "    <p>All content on pages in the UI are serialized POJOs.  In this case, it's a serialized array of beans with 2 properties, 'name' and 'description'.</p>", 
        "    <p>Other features (such as this aside) are added through annotations.</p>", 
        "</div>" 
    }, 
    asideFloat="RIGHT" 
) 
@SerializerConfig( 
    // For testing purposes, we want to use single quotes in all the serializers so it's easier to do simple 
    // String comparisons. 
    // You can apply any of the Serializer/Parser/BeanContext settings this way. 
    quoteChar="'" 
) 
public class RootResources extends BasicRestServletGroup { 
    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L; 
} 
```

This is what it looks like in a browser:

![Root Resources HTML](assets/images/restserveroverview-overview-rootresources-aaa77d0e6e65e2ce2ef746ef3cd23739_45276d5ab87a93c9.png)

![Root Resources JSON](assets/images/restserveroverview-overview-rootresources-json-61513da3ce4dc51807874fb369a0c4bd_f0133bd58b4c4d54.png)

The [HelloWorldResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/examples/rest/HelloWorldResource.html) class is our basic
example of a child REST resource:

```java
@Rest( 
    title="Hello World", 
    description="An example of the simplest-possible resource", 
    path="/helloWorld" 
) 
@HtmlDocConfig( 
    aside={ 
        "<div style='max-width:400px' class='text'>", 
        "    <p>This page shows a resource that simply response with a 'Hello world!' message</p>", 
        "    <p>The POJO serialized is a simple String.</p>", 
        "</div>" 
    } 
) 
public class HelloWorldResource extends BasicRestObject { 
 
    @RestGet(path="/*", summary="Responds with \"Hello world!\"") 
    public String sayHello() { 
        return "Hello world!"; 
    } 
} 
```

This is what it looks like in a browser:

![Hello World Resource HTML](assets/images/restserveroverview-overview-helloworldresource-813a321133b4b482cfdab78780b3c20a_42779c35ffd70d12.png)

It doesn't much simpler than that.
In this case, we're simply returning a string that will be converted to any of the supported languages (e.g.
JSON, XML, HTML, ...).
However, we could have returned any POJO consisting of beans, `Maps`, `Collections`, etc...

---

<a id="topics-restannotatedclassbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestAnnotatedClassBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 159 -->

# @Rest-Annotated Class Basics

The [@Rest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html) annotation is the primary way of defining and
configuring REST resource classes.

The functionality of the class itself is covered in detail in the topics later.

[Rest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html)
[allowedHeaderParams](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#allowedHeaderParams()) [allowedMethodHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#allowedMethodHeaders()) [allowedMethodParams](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#allowedMethodParams()) [beanStore](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#beanStore()) [callLogger](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#callLogger()) [children](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#children()) [clientVersionHeader](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#clientVersionHeader()) [config](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#config()) [consumes](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#consumes()) [converters](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#converters()) [debug](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#debug()) [debugEnablement](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#debugEnablement()) [debugOn](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#debugOn()) [defaultAccept](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#defaultAccept()) [defaultCharset](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#defaultCharset()) [defaultContentType](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#defaultContentType()) [defaultRequestAttributes](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#defaultRequestAttributes()) [defaultRequestHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#defaultRequestHeaders()) [defaultResponseHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#defaultResponseHeaders()) [description](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#description()) [disableContentParam](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#disableContentParam()) [encoders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#encoders()) [guards](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#guards()) [maxInput](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#maxInput()) [messages](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#messages()) [on](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#on()) [onClass](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#onClass()) [parsers](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#parsers()) [partParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#partParser()) [partSerializer](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#partSerializer()) [path](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#path()) [produces](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#produces()) [renderResponseStackTraces](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#renderResponseStackTraces()) [responseProcessors](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#responseProcessors()) [restChildrenClass](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#restChildrenClass()) [restOpArgs](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#restOpArgs()) [restOperationsClass](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#restOperationsClass()) [roleGuard](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#roleGuard()) [rolesDeclared](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#rolesDeclared()) [serializers](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#serializers()) [siteName](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#siteName()) [staticFiles](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#staticFiles()) [swagger](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#swagger()) [swaggerProvider](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#swaggerProvider()) [title](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#title()) [uriAuthority](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#uriAuthority()) [uriContext](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#uriContext()) [uriRelativity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#uriRelativity()) [uriResolution](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#uriResolution())

The [@Rest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html) annotation in inheritable from parents and
interfaces of resource classes.

When multiple annotations are defined at different levels, the annotation values are combined.
This is a particularly useful feature because it allows you to define your own configured parent resource classes that
can be extended by all your child resources so that they all share common settings.

---

<a id="topics-predefinedclasses"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/PredefinedClasses/ -->

<!-- page_index: 160 -->

# Predefined Classes

The following example represents the bare-minimum needed for deploying a top-level REST endpoint with basic JSON
marshalling support:

```java
@Rest( 
    path="/mypath", 
    serializers=JsonSerializer.class, 
    parsers=JsonParser.class 
) 
public class MyResource extends RestServlet { 
 
    @RestGet(path="/") 
    public Object getPojo() { 
        ... 
    } 
} 
```

The [RestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html) class provides all the logic for
starting up your REST application when the servlet container calls [init(ServletConfig)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html#<init>()).
On startup, it scans your class for annotations and sets up all of your serializers and parsers.
It then does this recursively for all child resources.

Users will typically not extend directly from [RestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html).
Instead, several classes are provided by the framework to provide additional functionality and to handle different
use-cases.

Users will typically extend from one of these `Basic*` classes:

[HttpServlet](https://jakarta.ee/specifications/servlet/6.0/apidocs/jakarta/servlet/http/HttpServlet.html)
[RestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html)
[BasicRestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestServlet.html) implements [BasicRestOperations](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestOperations.html), [BasicUniversalConfig](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicUniversalConfig.html)
[BasicRestServletGroup](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestServletGroup.html) implements [BasicGroupOperations](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicGroupOperations.html)
[SpringRestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/springboot/SpringRestServlet.html)
[BasicSpringRestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/springboot/BasicSpringRestServlet.html) implements [BasicRestOperations](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestOperations.html), [BasicUniversalConfig](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicUniversalConfig.html)
[BasicSpringRestServletGroup](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/springboot/BasicSpringRestServletGroup.html) implements [BasicGroupOperations](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicGroupOperations.html)
[RestObject](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestObject.html)
[BasicRestObject](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestObject.html) implements [BasicRestOperations](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestOperations.html), [BasicJsonConfig](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicJsonConfig.html)
[BasicRestObjectGroup](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestObjectGroup.html) implements [BasicGroupOperations](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicGroupOperations.html)

The [RestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html) class itself is not configured with any
serializers or parsers.
However, it does provide several convenience methods to be aware of:

[RestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html)Logging methods:[log(Level,String,Object...)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html#doLog(java.util.logging.Level,java.lang.Throwable,java.util.function.Supplier))[log(Level,Throwable,String,Object...)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html#doLog(java.util.logging.Level,java.lang.Throwable,java.util.function.Supplier))[log(String)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html#doLog(java.util.logging.Level,java.lang.Throwable,java.util.function.Supplier))[log(String,Throwable)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html#doLog(java.util.logging.Level,java.lang.Throwable,java.util.function.Supplier))Other methods:[getContext()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html#getContext())[getPath()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html#getPath())[getRequest()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html#getRequest())[getResponse()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/RestServlet.html#getResponse())

The `Basic*` classes implement the [BasicRestOperations](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestOperations.html) interface which defines common endpoints for swagger documentation, statistics, and serving static files:

```java
public interface BasicRestOperations { 
 
    @RestGet(path="/api/*") 
    public Swagger getSwagger(RestRequest req); 
 
    @RestGet(path="/htdocs/*") 
    public HttpResource getHtdoc(@Path String path, Locale locale); 
 
    @RestGet(path="favicon.ico") 
    public HttpResource getFavIcon(); 
 
    @RestGet(path="/stats") 
    public RestContextStats getStats(RestRequest req); 
 
    @RestOp(method=ANY, path="/error") 
    public void error(); 
} 
```

The `Basic*` classes also implement [BasicJsonConfig](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicJsonConfig.html)
interface which provides basic JSON marshalling support.
Other config interfaces are available as well to quickly provide different types of marshalling support.
Note that these interfaces do not define any methods but rather simply provide a set of commonly-used annotations so
that you don't need to define them on all your classes.

[BasicJsonConfig](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicJsonConfig.html)
[BasicJsonHtmlConfig](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicJsonHtmlConfig.html)
[BasicJson5Config](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicJson5Config.html)
[BasicOpenApiConfig](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicOpenApiConfig.html)
[BasicUniversalConfig](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicUniversalConfig.html)

For example, if you want to provide a resource that supports all languages in Juneau, simply add the [BasicUniversalConfig](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/config/BasicUniversalConfig.html) interface like so:

```java
@Rest(...) 
public class MyResource extends RestServlet implements BasicUniversalConfig { 
    ... 
} 
```

The `*Group` classes implement the [BasicGroupOperations](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicGroupOperations.html) interface which provides an additional REST endpoint for listing and navigating child resources:

```java
public interface BasicGroupOperations { 
 
    @RestGet(path="/") 
    public ChildResourceDescriptions getChildren(RestRequest req); 
} 
```

The `*Spring*` classes are meant to be used in Spring Boot environments so that you can take full advantage of the
Spring Framework for injecting dependencies on child resources and helper classes.

The `*Object*` classes provide the same functionality as the servlet classes but do not extend from [HttpServlet](https://jakarta.ee/specifications/servlet/6.0/apidocs/jakarta/servlet/http/HttpServlet.html).
This becomes important in Spring Boot environments where you may want to define child resources as Spring Beans but
don't want Spring Boot to auto-detect them as servlets to be deployed as top-level resources.
This is less important in standard servlet containers that don't auto-deploy servlets.
In those environments, you can also use servlet classes for child resources.

The following is a breakdown of which classes you will use in different cases:

**Top level resources in a servlet container:**

[BasicRestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestServlet.html)
[BasicRestServletGroup](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestServletGroup.html)

**Top level resources in a Spring Boot environment:**

[BasicSpringRestServlet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/springboot/BasicSpringRestServlet.html)
[BasicSpringRestServletGroup](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/springboot/BasicSpringRestServletGroup.html)

**Child resources:**

[BasicRestObject](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestObject.html)
[BasicRestObjectGroup](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestObjectGroup.html)

---

<a id="topics-childresources"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ChildResources/ -->

<!-- page_index: 161 -->

# Child Resources

Child Resources are REST servlets or objects that are linked to parent resources through the [@Rest(children)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#children()) annotation.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> /** Parent Resource */
> @Rest(
>     path="/parent",
>     children={MyChildResource.class}
> )
> public MyRootResources extends BasicRestServletGroup {...}
> ```
>
> ```java
> /** Child Resource */
> @Rest(
>     path="/child"  // Path relative to parent resource.
> )
> // Note that we don't need to extend from RestServlet.
> public MyChildResource extends BasicRestObject {...}
> ```

The path of the child resource gets appended to the path of the parent resource.
So in the example above, the child resource is accessed through the URL `/parent/child`.

One advantage of using child resources is that they do not need to be declared in the JEE `web.xml` file.

Initialization of and access to the child resources occurs through the parent resource.

Children can be nested arbitrary deep to create complex REST interfaces with a single top-level REST servlet.

As explained earlier, child REST objects typically extend from [BasicRestObject](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestObject.html) or [BasicRestObjectGroup](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/servlet/BasicRestObjectGroup.html) and not from one of the servlet classes.
They also technically don't even need to extend from those classes and can instead just be a normal class annotated with
the bare-minimum [@Rest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html) and [@RestOp](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html) annotations.

---

<a id="topics-pathvariables"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/PathVariables/ -->

<!-- page_index: 162 -->

# Path Variables

The path can contain variables that get resolved to [@Path](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Path.html)
parameters or access through the [RestRequest.getPathParams()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestRequest.html#getPathParams()) method.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @Rest(
>     path="/myResource/{foo}/{bar}"
> )
> public class MyResource extends BasicRestServlet {
>
>     @RestPost("/{baz}")
>     public void String doX(@Path String foo, @Path int bar) {
>         ...
>     }
> }
> ```

Variables can be used on either top-level or child resources and can be defined on multiple levels.

Path variables resolved in parent resource paths are also available to the child resources.

> [!NOTE]
> All variables in the path must be specified or else the target will not resolve and a `404` will result.

The path remainder (the part matched by `/*`) can be captured using either [@Path("/\*")](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Path.html) or the more intuitive [@PathRemainder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/PathRemainder.html) annotation.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @Rest
> public class MyResource extends BasicRestServlet {
>
>     // Using @PathRemainder (preferred)
>     @RestGet("/files/*")
>     public File getFile(@PathRemainder String path) {
>         return new File(path);
>     }
>
>     // Equivalent using @Path("/*")
>     @RestPost("/upload/*")
>     public void uploadFile(@Path("/*") String path, @Content File file) {
>         ...
>     }
> }
> ```

The [@PathRemainder](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/PathRemainder.html) annotation supports the same features as [@Path](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Path.html), including:

- Default values via `def()` property
- Custom parsers and serializers
- Schema validation
- OpenAPI/Swagger documentation generation

---

<a id="topics-deployment"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Deployment/ -->

<!-- page_index: 163 -->

# Deployment

> [!NOTE]
> When deployed as a child of another resource, you MAY extend from one of the servlet classes but it's not necessary.
> The only requirement is that the class be annotated with @Rest and have one of the following constructors if they aren't
> already Spring Beans:
>
> - `public T()`
> - `public T(RestContext.Builder)`

---

<a id="topics-lifecyclehooks"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/LifecycleHooks/ -->

<!-- page_index: 164 -->

# Lifecycle Hooks

Lifecycle hooks allow you to hook into lifecycle events of the servlet/resource creation and REST calls.

For example, if you want to add an initialization method to your resource:

```java
@Rest(...) 
public class MyResource extends BasicRestObject { 
 
    // Our database. 
    private Map myDatabase; 
 
    @RestInit 
    public void initMyDatabase(RestContext.Builder builder) throws Exception { 
        myDatabase = new LinkedHashMap(); 
    } 
} 
```

Or if you want to intercept REST calls:

```java
@Rest(...) 
public class MyResource extends BasicRestObject { 
 
    // Add a request attribute to all incoming requests. 
    @RestPreCall 
    public void onPreCall(RestRequest req) { 
        req.setAttribute("foo", "bar"); 
    } 
} 
```

The following lifecycle annotations are provided:

Resource lifecycle events:[RestInit](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestInit.html) - Right before initialization.[RestPostInit](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestPostInit.html) - Right after initialization.[RestDestroy](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestDestroy.html) - Right before servlet destroy.REST call lifecycle events:[RestStartCall](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestStartCall.html) - At the beginning of a REST call.[RestPreCall](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestPreCall.html) - Right before the @RestOp method is invoked.[RestPostCall](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestPostCall.html) - Right after the @RestOp method is invoked.[RestEndCall](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestEndCall.html) - At the end of the REST call after the response has been flushed.

---

<a id="topics-restopannotatedmethodbasics"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestOpAnnotatedMethodBasics/ -->

<!-- page_index: 165 -->

# @RestOp-Annotated Method Basics

REST Java methods are identified on REST servlets using the [@RestOp](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html) annotation.

The annotation allows the framework to identify the available REST methods through reflection.

[RestOp](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html)
[clientVersion](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#clientVersion()) [consumes](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#consumes()) [converters](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#converters()) [debug](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#debug()) [defaultAccept](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#defaultAccept()) [defaultCharset](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#defaultCharset()) [defaultContentType](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#defaultContentType()) [defaultRequestAttributes](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#defaultRequestAttributes()) [defaultRequestFormData](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#defaultRequestFormData()) [defaultRequestHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#defaultRequestHeaders()) [defaultRequestQueryData](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#defaultRequestQueryData()) [defaultResponseHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#defaultResponseHeaders()) [description](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#description()) [encoders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#encoders()) [guards](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#guards()) [matchers](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#matchers()) [maxInput](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#maxInput()) [method](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#method()) [on](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#on()) [parsers](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#parsers()) [path](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#path()) [produces](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#produces()) [roleGuard](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#roleGuard()) [rolesDeclared](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#rolesDeclared()) [serializers](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#serializers()) [summary](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#summary()) [swagger](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#swagger()) [value](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#value())

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @RestOp(method=GET, path="/")
> public String sayHello() {
>     return "Hello world!";
> }
> ```

The following specialized annotations are also provided for specific HTTP methods:

[RestGet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestGet.html) [RestPut](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestPut.html) [RestPost](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestPost.html) [RestPatch](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestPatch.html) [RestDelete](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestDelete.html) [RestOptions](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOptions.html)

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @RestGet(path="/")
> public String sayHello() {
>     return "Hello world!";
> }
> ```

---

<a id="topics-inferredhttpmethodsandpaths"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/InferredHttpMethodsAndPaths/ -->

<!-- page_index: 166 -->

# Inferred HTTP Methods and Paths

When the `name` and/or `path` values are not specified, their values are inferred from the Java method name.

The HTTP method can be inferred from the Java method by starting the method name with any of the following:

- `get`
- `put`
- `post`
- `delete`
- `options`
- `head`
- `trace`
- `patch`

If `path` is not defined, it's inferred from the Java method name (minus the prefix above).

> [!TIP]
> **Examples**
> ```java
> // Method="GET", path="/foo"
> @RestOp
> public String getFoo() {...}
> ```
>
> ```java
> // Method="DELETE", path="/foo"
> @RestOp
> public String deleteFoo() {...}
> ```
>
> ```java
> // Method="GET", path="/foo"
> // "GET" is default
> @RestOp
> public String foo() {...}
> ```
>
> ```java
> // Method="GET", path="/"
> @RestOp(path="/")
> public String foo() {...}
> ```
>
> ```java
> // Method="GET", path="/"
> @RestOp
> public String get() {...}
> ```
>
> ```java
> // Method="POST", path="/"
> @RestOp
> public String post() {...}
> ```

> [!NOTE]
> If `name` and `path` are both specified, the Java method name can be anything.

---

Java methods can contain any of the following parameters in any order:

**Parameters based on class types:****Request objects:**`AsyncContext` [CookieList](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/arg/CookieList.html) `DispatcherType` [HttpPartParserSession](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/httppart/HttpPartParserSession.html) [HttpPartSerializerSession](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/httppart/HttpPartSerializerSession.html) `HttpServletRequest` [InputStream](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/io/InputStream.html) [InputStreamParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/InputStreamParser.html) [Locale](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/Locale.html) [Messages](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/cp/Messages.html) [Parser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/Parser.html) [Principal](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/security/Principal.html) [Reader](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/io/Reader.html) [ReaderParser](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/ReaderParser.html) [RequestAttributes](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestAttributes.html) [RequestContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestContent.html) [RequestHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestHeaders.html) [RequestPathParams](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestPathParams.html) [ResourceBundle](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/util/ResourceBundle.html) [UriContext](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/UriContext.html) [UriResolver](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/UriResolver.html) [VarResolverSession](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/svl/VarResolverSession.html)**Response objects:**`HttpServletResponse` [OutputStream](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/io/OutputStream.html) [RestResponse](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestResponse.html) `ServletOutputStream` [Writer](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/io/Writer.html)**Session objects:**`HttpSession` [RestSession](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestSession.html) [UrlPath](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/util/UrlPath.html) [TE](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/TE.html) [Thrown](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Thrown.html) [Upgrade](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Upgrade.html) [UserAgent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/UserAgent.html) [Warning](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/header/Warning.html)**Context values:**[BeanContext](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/BeanContext.html) [CallLogger](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/logger/CallLogger.html) [Config](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/config/Config.html) [JsonSchemaGenerator](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/jsonschema/JsonSchemaGenerator.html) [MethodExecStore](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/stats/MethodExecStore.html) [ParserSet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/ParserSet.html) [RestChildren](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestChildren.html) [RestContext](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestContext.html) [RestContextStats](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/stats/RestContextStats.html) [RestOpContext](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestOpContext.html) [RestOperations](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestOperations.html) [SerializerSet](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/serializer/SerializerSet.html) [StaticFiles](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/staticfile/StaticFiles.html) [ThrownStore](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/stats/ThrownStore.html)**Annotated parameters:**[Attr](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Attr.html) [Content](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Content.html) [Path](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Path.html) [FormData](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/FormData.html) [HasFormData](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/HasFormData.html) [Query](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Query.html) [HasQuery](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/HasQuery.html) [Header](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Header.html) [StatusCode](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/StatusCode.html) [Method](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Method.html) [Request](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Request.html) [Response](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Response.html)

In Spring Boot environments, any available Spring Beans can also be passed in as parameters.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @RestGet("/example1/{a1}/{a2}/{a3}/*")
> public String doGetExample1(
>     RestRequest req,
>     RestResponse res,
>     @Method String method,
>     @Path("a1") String a1,
>     @Path("a2") int a2,
>     @Path("a3") UUID a3,
>     @Query("p1") int p1,
>     @Query("p2") String p2,
>     @Query("p3") UUID p3,
>     @HasQuery("p3") boolean hasP3,
>     @Path("/*") String remainder,
>     @Header("Accept-Language") String lang,
>     @Header("Accept") String accept,
>     @Header("DNT") int doNotTrack,
>     RequestAttributes attributes,
>     ResourceBundle nls
> ) {
>     // Do something with all of those
> }
> ```

Additional parameter types can be defined via the annotation [Rest.restOpArgs()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#restOpArgs()) or by calling [restOpArgs()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestContext.Builder.html#restOpArgs(java.lang.Class...)).

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @Rest(
>     restOpArgs={ MyOpArg.class }  // Option #1 - Via annotation
> )
> public class MyResource extends BasicRestObject {
>
>     // Option #2 - Programmatically
>     @RestInit
>     public void init(RestContext.Builder builder) {
>         builder.restOpArgs(MyOpArg.class);
>     }
> }
> ```

---

<a id="topics-javamethodreturntypes"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JavaMethodReturnTypes/ -->

<!-- page_index: 168 -->

# Java Method Return Types

The return type of the Java method can be any serializable POJO as defined in [POJO Categories](#topics-pojocategories).

It can also be void if the method is not sending any output (e.g a request redirect) or is setting the output using the [RestResponse.setContent(Object)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestResponse.html#setContent(java.lang.Object)) method.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @RestGet
> public String doGet() {
>     return "Hello World!";
> }
> ```

In addition to POJOs, the following return types are also supported:

[InputStream](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/io/InputStream.html) [Reader](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/io/Reader.html)
[HttpEntity](https://hc.apache.org/httpcomponents-core-4.4.x/current/httpcore/apidocs/org/apache/http/HttpEntity.html) [HttpResource](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/resource/HttpResource.html) [HttpResponse](https://hc.apache.org/httpcomponents-core-4.4.x/current/httpcore/apidocs/org/apache/http/HttpResponse.html)
[Accepted](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Accepted.html) [AlreadyReported](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/AlreadyReported.html) [BadRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/BadRequest.html) [Conflict](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Conflict.html) [Continue](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Continue.html) [Created](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Created.html) [EarlyHints](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/EarlyHints.html) [ExpectationFailed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/ExpectationFailed.html) [FailedDependency](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/FailedDependency.html) [Forbidden](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Forbidden.html) [Found](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Found.html) [Gone](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Gone.html) [HttpVersionNotSupported](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/HttpVersionNotSupported.html) [IMUsed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/IMUsed.html) [InsufficientStorage](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/InsufficientStorage.html) [InternalServerError](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/InternalServerError.html) [LengthRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/LengthRequired.html) [Locked](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Locked.html) [LoopDetected](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/LoopDetected.html) [MethodNotAllowed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MethodNotAllowed.html) [MisdirectedRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MisdirectedRequest.html) [MovedPermanently](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MovedPermanently.html) [MultipleChoices](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MultipleChoices.html) [MultiStatus](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MultiStatus.html) [NetworkAuthenticationRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NetworkAuthenticationRequired.html) [NoContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NoContent.html) [NonAuthoritiveInformation](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NonAuthoritiveInformation.html) [NotAcceptable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotAcceptable.html) [NotExtended](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotExtended.html) [NotFound](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotFound.html) [NotImplemented](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotImplemented.html) [NotModified](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotModified.html) [Ok](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Ok.html) [PartialContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PartialContent.html) [PayloadTooLarge](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PayloadTooLarge.html) [PermanentRedirect](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PermanentRedirect.html) [PreconditionFailed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PreconditionFailed.html) [PreconditionRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PreconditionRequired.html) [Processing](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Processing.html) [RangeNotSatisfiable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/RangeNotSatisfiable.html) [RequestHeaderFieldsTooLarge](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/RequestHeaderFieldsTooLarge.html) [ResetContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/ResetContent.html) [SeeOther](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/SeeOther.html) [ServiceUnavailable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/ServiceUnavailable.html) [SwitchingProtocols](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/SwitchingProtocols.html) [TemporaryRedirect](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/TemporaryRedirect.html) [TooManyRequests](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/TooManyRequests.html) [Unauthorized](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Unauthorized.html) [UnavailableForLegalReasons](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UnavailableForLegalReasons.html) [UnprocessableEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UnprocessableEntity.html) [UnsupportedMediaType](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UnsupportedMediaType.html) [UpgradeRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UpgradeRequired.html) [UriTooLong](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UriTooLong.html) [UseProxy](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UseProxy.html) [VariantAlsoNegotiates](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/VariantAlsoNegotiates.html)
[ChildResourceDescriptions](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/beans/ChildResourceDescriptions.html) [ResourceDescriptions](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/beans/ResourceDescriptions.html) [SeeOtherRoot](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/beans/SeeOtherRoot.html) [Throwable](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Throwable.html)
[Response](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Response.html)

REST Java methods can also generate a response via the following:

- By calling [RestResponse.setContent(Object)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestResponse.html#setContent(java.lang.Object)) with any of the types above.
- By accessing the [Writer](https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/io/Writer.html) directly by calling [RestResponse.getNegotiatedWriter()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestResponse.html#getNegotiatedWriter()) and writing the output yourself.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // Equivalent method 1
> @RestGet("/example1/{personId}")
> public Person doGet1(@Path("personId") UUID personId) {
>     Person person = getPersonById(personId);
>     return person;
> }
>
> // Equivalent method 2
> @RestGet("/example2/{personId}")
> public void doGet2(RestResponse res, @Path("personId") UUID personId) {
>     Person person = getPersonById(personId);
>     res.setContent(person);
> }
> ```

Additional parameter types can be defined via the annotation [Rest.responseProcessors()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#responseProcessors()) or by calling [responseProcessors()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestContext.Builder.html#responseProcessors(java.lang.Class...)).

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @Rest(
>     responseProcessors={ MyResponseProcessor.class }  // Option #1 - Via annotation
> )
> public class MyResource extends BasicRestObject {
>
>     // Option #2 - Programmatically
>     @RestInit
>     public void init(RestContext.Builder builder) {
>         builder.responseProcessors(MyResponseProcessor.class);
>     }
> }
> ```

---

<a id="topics-javamethodthrowabletypes"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JavaMethodThrowableTypes/ -->

<!-- page_index: 169 -->

# Java Method Throwable Types

Annotated Java methods can throw any of the following:

[BadRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/BadRequest.html) [Conflict](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Conflict.html) [ExpectationFailed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/ExpectationFailed.html) [FailedDependency](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/FailedDependency.html) [Forbidden](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Forbidden.html) [Gone](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Gone.html) [HttpVersionNotSupported](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/HttpVersionNotSupported.html) [InsufficientStorage](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/InsufficientStorage.html) [InternalServerError](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/InternalServerError.html) [LengthRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/LengthRequired.html) [Locked](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Locked.html) [LoopDetected](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/LoopDetected.html) [MethodNotAllowed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MethodNotAllowed.html) [MisdirectedRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/MisdirectedRequest.html) [NetworkAuthenticationRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NetworkAuthenticationRequired.html) [NotAcceptable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotAcceptable.html) [NotExtended](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotExtended.html) [NotFound](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotFound.html) [NotImplemented](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/NotImplemented.html) [PayloadTooLarge](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PayloadTooLarge.html) [PreconditionFailed](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PreconditionFailed.html) [PreconditionRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/PreconditionRequired.html) [RangeNotSatisfiable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/RangeNotSatisfiable.html) [RequestHeaderFieldsTooLarge](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/RequestHeaderFieldsTooLarge.html) [ServiceUnavailable](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/ServiceUnavailable.html) [TooManyRequests](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/TooManyRequests.html) [Unauthorized](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/Unauthorized.html) [UnavailableForLegalReasons](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UnavailableForLegalReasons.html) [UnprocessableEntity](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UnprocessableEntity.html) [UnsupportedMediaType](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UnsupportedMediaType.html) [UpgradeRequired](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UpgradeRequired.html) [UriTooLong](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/UriTooLong.html) [VariantAlsoNegotiates](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/response/VariantAlsoNegotiates.html)
[Response](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/http/annotation/Response.html)

All other throwables get processed as follows:

[ParseException](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/parser/ParseException.html)
[InvalidDataConversionException](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/InvalidDataConversionException.html)

- Any class named "*AccessDenied*" or "*Unauthorized*"

- Any class named "*Empty*" or "*NotFound*"

Anything else processed as 500/Internal Server Error.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> @RestGet("/user/login")
> public Ok login(
>     @FormData("username") String username,
>     @FormData("password") String password
> ) throws Unauthorized
> {
>     if (! isOK(username, password))
>         throw new Unauthorized("You're not welcome!");
>
>     return Ok.OK;
> }
> ```

---

<a id="topics-pathpatterns"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/PathPatterns/ -->

<!-- page_index: 170 -->

# Path Patterns

The [@RestOp(path)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#path()) annotation allows you to define
URL path patterns to match against.
These patterns can contain variables of the form `{xxx}` that can be passed in directly to the Java methods as extra
parameters.

In the following example, 3 separate GET request handlers are defined with different path patterns.
Note how the variables are passed in as additional arguments on the method, and how those arguments are automatically
converted to the specified class type...

```java
// Default method 
@RestGet(path="/*") 
public void doGetDefault() { 
    ... 
} 
 
// Method with path pattern 
@RestGet(path="/xxx") 
public void doGetNoArgs(...) { 
    ... 
} 
 
// Method with path pattern with arguments 
@RestGet(path="/xxx/{foo}/{bar}/{baz}/{bing}") 
public void doGetWithArgs( 
        @Path("foo") String foo, 
        @Path("bar") int bar, 
        @Path("baz") MyEnum baz, 
        @Path("bing") UUID qux 
) { 
    ... 
} 
```

By default, path patterns are matched using a best-match heuristic.

When overlaps occur, URLs are matched from most-specific to most-general order:

```java
// Try first 
@RestGet("/foo/bar") 
public void method1() { 
    ... 
} 
 
// Try second 
@RestGet("/foo/{bar}") 
public void method2(...) { 
    ... 
} 
 
// Try third 
@RestGet("/foo/*") 
public void method3(...) { 
    ... 
} 
 
// Try last 
@RestGet("/*") 
public void method4(...) { 
    ... 
} 
```

Paths that end with `/*` will do a prefix match on the incoming URL.

Any remainder after the match can be accessed through [RequestPathParams.getRemainder()](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestPathParams.html#getRemainder()) or parameters with the `@Path("/*")` annotation.

On the other hand, paths that don't end with `/*` (e.g. `/` or `/foo`) will require an exact URL match, and if any
remainder exists, a 404 (not found) error will be thrown.

The following example shows the distinction.

```java
@RestGet("/*") 
public void doGet(@Path("/*") String remainder) { 
    // URL path pattern can have remainder accessible through req.getRemainder(). 
} 
 
@RestPut("/") 
public void doPut() { 
    // URL path pattern must match exactly and will cause a 404 error if a remainder exists. 
} 
```

---

<a id="topics-matchers"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Matchers/ -->

<!-- page_index: 171 -->

# Matchers

[RestMatchers](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/matcher/RestMatcher.html) are used to allow multiple Java methods to
be tied to the same HTTP method and path but differentiated by some request attribute such as a specific header value.

> [!TIP]
> **Example**
> ```java
> // GET method that gets invoked for administrators
> @RestGet(path="/*", matchers=IsAdminMatcher.class)
> public Object doGetForAdmin() {
>     ...
> }
>
> // GET method that gets invoked for everyone else
> @RestGet("/*")
> public Object doGetForEveryoneElse() {
>     ...
> }
> ```

The interface for matchers is simple:

```java
public class IsAdminMatcher extends RestMatcher { 
 
    @Override /* RestMatcher */ 
    public boolean matches(RestRequest req) { 
        return req.isUserInRole("ADMINS_GROUP"); 
    } 
} 
```

> [!NOTE]
> - If no methods are found with a matching matcher, a 412 Precondition Failed status is returned.
> - If multiple matchers are specified on the same method, ONLY ONE matcher needs to match for the method to be invoked.
> - Note that you CANNOT define identical paths on different methods UNLESS you use matchers.
>   That includes paths that are only different in variable names (e.g. `/foo/{bar}` and `/foo/{baz}`).
>   If you try to do so, a ServletException will be thrown on startup.
> - Methods with matchers take precedence over methods without.
>   Otherwise, methods are attempted in the order they appear in the class.

> [!NOTE]
> **See Also**
> [RestOp](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html)
> [matchers](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/RestOp.html#matchers())

The following matchers are provided:

[RestMatcher](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/matcher/RestMatcher.html)
[MultipartFormDataMatcher](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/matcher/MultipartFormDataMatcher.html)
[UrlEncodedFormMatcher](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/matcher/UrlEncodedFormMatcher.html)

---

<a id="topics-overloadinghttpmethods"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/OverloadingHttpMethods/ -->

<!-- page_index: 172 -->

# Overloading HTTP Methods

Through the use of the built-in `method` GET parameter, you can implement requests beyond the basic REST http method
types.

For example, the URL `/sample/foo?method=BAR` will cause the following method to be invoked...

```java
@RestOp(method="BAR") 
public void doBar(RestRequest req, RestResponse res) { 
    // Handle BAR requests 
} 
```

To support overloaded methods, the [@Rest(allowedMethodParams)](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/annotation/Rest.html#allowedMethodParams()) setting must be enabled on your servlet.

```java
@Rest( 
    // Allow &method parameter on BAR requests 
    allowedMethodParams="BAR" 
) 
```

---

<a id="topics-additionalinformation"></a>

<!-- source_url: https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/AdditionalInformation/ -->

<!-- page_index: 173 -->

# Additional Information

Refer to the following Javadocs for more information:

[RestRequest](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestRequest.html)
[RestResponse](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/RestResponse.html)
[RequestContent](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestContent.html)
[RequestHeaders](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestHeaders.html)
[RequestQueryParams](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestQueryParams.html)
[RequestFormParams](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestFormParams.html)
[RequestPathParams](https://juneau.apache.org/site/apidocs/org/apache/juneau/rest/httppart/RequestPathParams.html)
